{{wikidata link|Q11537594}} == Japanese content == {{座標一覧}} '''桑田神社'''（くわだじんじゃ、くわた-）は、『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』に記載されている{{jalink|丹波国|Q954511}}{{jalink|桑田郡|Q11537600}}の{{jalink|式内社}}。以下の二社が{{jalink|論社|Q135022904}}とされている。 * 1. {{jalink|桑田神社#桑田神社 (亀岡市篠町山本)|Q11537594|桑田神社}} （{{jalink|京都府|Q120730}}{{jalink|亀岡市|Q633996}}篠町山本北絛50.51） - 旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} * 2. {{jalink|桑田神社#桑田神社 (亀岡市篠町馬堀)|Q11537594|桑田神社}} （京都府亀岡市篠町馬堀東垣内3） - 旧{{jalink|無格社}} === 概要 === 『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』に「{{jalink|丹波国|Q954511}}{{jalink|桑田郡|Q11537600}} 桑田神社」と記載される{{jalink|式内社}}。 亀岡市内に論社が二社ある。馬堀の社は山本の社の元宮ともいわれるが、正確な由緒は不詳。 両社とも{{jalink|亀岡市|Q633996}}東部、大堰川右岸の保津峡入り口近くに鎮座し、川向うには{{jalink|請田神社|Q11631810}}が鎮座する。 === 桑田神社 (亀岡市篠町山本) === {{神社 |名称 = 桑田神社 |画像 = [[File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) keidai.JPG|280px]]
拝殿（左）と本殿（右奥） |所在地 = {{jalink|京都府|Q120730}}{{jalink|亀岡市|Q633996}}篠町山本北絛50.51 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|00|51.83|N|135|36|33.88|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|name=桑田神社 (亀岡市篠町山本)}} |祭神 = {{jalink|イチキシマヒメ|Q11059454|市杵島姫命}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（小）{{jalink|論社|Q135022904}}
旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = {{jalink|流造|Q210684}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|10月25日|Q2954}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20160304122457/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/tanba/bun/tab380102-01.html 桑田神社（亀岡市篠町山本北条）]（延喜式神社の調査［個人サイト］）。 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=桑田神社|coordinates= {{coord2|35|00|51.83|N|135|36|33.88|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|00|51.83|N|135|36|33.88|E}}}} }} [[File:Hozukyō (Kameoka).JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{jalink|保津峡|Q10887680}}入り口
右岸を走る{{jalink|嵯峨野線|Q1073011}}の奥に当社。左岸には{{jalink|請田神社|Q11631810}}が鎮座する。]] '''桑田神社'''（くわだじんじゃ、くわた-）は、{{jalink|京都府|Q120730}}{{jalink|亀岡市|Q633996}}篠町山本にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}{{jalink|論社|Q135022904}}。{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。 === 祭神 === ; 主祭神 * '''{{jalink|イチキシマヒメ|Q11059454|市杵島姫命}}''' （いちきしまひめのみこと） ; 配祀神 * {{jalink|大山咋神|Q10935105|大山咋命}} （おおやまくいのみこと） * {{jalink|オオヤマツミ|Q386563|大山祇命}} （おおやまつみのみこと） === 歴史 === 祭神の大山咋命・市杵島姫命は、{{jalink|鍬山神社|Q11650775}}（亀岡市上矢田町）祭神とともに、湖であった{{jalink|亀岡盆地|Q11181997}}を開拓のため{{jalink|保津峡|Q10887680}}を開削したといわれる。当地は盆地入り口にあたるため政治的にも重要な地で、{{jalink|秦氏|Q987290}}の勢力がいち早く進出したとされる。社伝では、この地は「田庭」と呼ばれており「丹波」はこの田庭より名付けられたとされる『亀岡神社誌』桑田神社項。{{Efn|「丹波」は古くは「たには・たにわ」と呼ばれたことは『和名抄』から分かっており、考古学的にも語源として「田庭」とする説がある（「{{jalink|丹波国#「丹波」の名称と由来|Q954511}}」を参照）。}}。その後、田庭には早稲を作ったり桑を植えて養蚕を営んだことに因み、田庭を今でも小字で「桑の木」と呼んでいると伝える。 {{jalink|天明|Q1194121}}年間（{{jalink|1781年|Q7719}}-{{jalink|1789年|Q7772}}）社殿が焼失。 当地の地名が「桑田」であることは『大日本史神祇志』にも記されているが、明治6年まで川向うの神社と同じく「請田神社（浮田神社）」を名乗っていた境内説明板より。。明治6年6月、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534}}において{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}に列し、明治10年6月に式内社桑田神社を公称。 === 境内 === File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) honden.JPG|本殿 File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) sandō.JPG|参道 File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) torii.JPG|鳥居 === 摂末社 === * 地主神社 * 五社神社 * 天満宮 * 水神宮 * 九頭龍大神 * 秋葉宮 * 祓戸宮 === 現地情報 === ; 所在地 * {{jalink|京都府|Q120730}}{{jalink|亀岡市|Q633996}}篠町山本北絛50.51 ; 交通アクセス * 最寄駅：{{jalink|西日本旅客鉄道|Q502125}}（JR西日本）{{jalink|嵯峨野線|Q1073011}} {{jalink|馬堀駅|Q1044103}} （徒歩約13分） ; 周辺 * {{jalink|請田神社|Q11631810}} === 桑田神社 (亀岡市篠町馬堀) === {{神社 |名称 = 桑田神社 |画像 = [[File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō umahori, Kameoka) keidai.JPG|250px]]
中央に本殿、右隣に天満宮 |所在地 = {{jalink|京都府|Q120730}}{{jalink|亀岡市|Q633996}}篠町馬堀東垣内3 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|00|31.38|N|135|36|08.36|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|name=桑田神社 (亀岡市篠町馬堀)}} |祭神 = {{jalink|イチキシマヒメ|Q11059454|市杵島姫命}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（小）{{jalink|論社|Q135022904}}
旧{{jalink|無格社}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20160304122202/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/tanba/bun/tab380102-02.html 桑田神社（亀岡市篠町馬堀東垣内）]（延喜式神社の調査［個人サイト］）。 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|10月25日|Q2954}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=桑田神社|coordinates= {{coord2|35|00|31.38|N|135|36|08.36|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|00|31.38|N|135|36|08.36|E}}}} }} '''桑田神社'''（くわだじんじゃ、くわた-）は、{{jalink|京都府|Q120730}}{{jalink|亀岡市|Q633996}}篠町馬堀にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}{{jalink|論社|Q135022904}}。{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|無格社}}（社格がないという意味ではなく「無格社」という社格）。 === 祭神 === * '''{{jalink|イチキシマヒメ|Q11059454|市杵島姫命}}''' （いちきしまひめのみこと） === 歴史 === 由緒は不詳。当地から篠町山本に祭神が遷ったともいわれる[https://tanbarakuichi.sakura.ne.jp/shrine/kameoka/11.html#k106 馬堀桑田神社]（丹波の神社［個人サイト］）。。 === 境内 === File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō umahori, Kameoka) honden.JPG|本殿の覆屋 File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō umahori, Kameoka) torii.JPG|鳥居 === 摂末社 === * 天満宮 === 現地情報 === ; 所在地 * {{jalink|京都府|Q120730}}{{jalink|亀岡市|Q633996}}篠町馬堀東垣内3 ; 交通アクセス * 最寄駅：{{jalink|西日本旅客鉄道|Q502125}}（JR西日本）{{jalink|嵯峨野線|Q1073011}} {{jalink|馬堀駅|Q1044103}} （徒歩約3分） === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Notelist}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} === 参考文献 === {{Commons|Category:Kuwada-jinja (Kameoka)}} * 境内説明板（篠町山本のみ） * 『日本歴史地名大系 京都府の地名』（{{jalink|平凡社|Q11482108}}）亀岡市 桑田神社項 * 式内社研究会編『式内社調査報告 第18巻』（皇學館大学出版部）桑田神社項 {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くわたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kuwada Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kuwada Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:丹波国の式内社]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:京都府の旧無格社]] [[Category:丹波湖伝説]] [[Category:亀岡市の建築物]] [[Category:need translation]]

{{wikidata link|Q11537594}} == Japanese content == {{Coordinates list}} '''Kuwada Shrine''' (くわだじんじゃ / くわたじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} listed in the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}}'' for Kuwata District, {{jalink|Tanba Province|Q954511}}. The following two shrines are treated as candidate shrines ({{jalink|ronsha|Q135022904}}): * 1. {{jalink|Kuwada Shrine#Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto, Kameoka)|Q11537594|Kuwada Shrine}} (Shinocho Yamamoto, {{jalink|Kameoka|Q633996}}, {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}) - former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} * 2. {{jalink|Kuwada Shrine#Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka)|Q11537594|Kuwada Shrine}} (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka, Kyoto) - former {{jalink|mukakusha}} === Overview === The ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' records "Kuwada Shrine, Kuwata District, Tanba Province." Two candidate shrines survive in Kameoka City. The Umahori shrine is sometimes said to be the former original site of the Yamamoto shrine, but the exact history is unclear. Both are in eastern Kameoka near the entrance to {{jalink|Hozukyo Gorge|Q10887680}} on the right bank of the Oi River; {{jalink|Uketa Shrine|Q11631810}} stands across the river. === Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto, Kameoka) === {{Shrine | name = Kuwada Shrine | image = [[File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) keidai.JPG|280px]]
Haiden (left) and honden (rear right) | location = {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}{{jalink|Kameoka|Q633996}} Shinocho Yamamoto Kitajo 50.51 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|00|51.83|N|135|36|33.88|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|name=Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto, Kameoka)}} | deity = {{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} | status = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small), {{jalink|ronsha|Q135022904}}
former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} | founded = Unknown | architecture = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | annual_festival = {{jalink|October 25|Q2954}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20160304122457/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/tanba/bun/tab380102-01.html Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto Kitajo, Kameoka)] (private research site). | map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Kuwada Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|35|00|51.83|N|135|36|33.88|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|00|51.83|N|135|36|33.88|E}}}} }} '''Kuwada Shrine''' in Yamamoto is a Shinto shrine in Kameoka, Kyoto. It is a candidate Engishiki shrine; its former rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} ; Additional deities * {{jalink|Oyamakui|Q10935105|Oyamakui-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Oyamazumi|Q386563|Oyamazumi-no-Mikoto}} === History === Tradition says the deities, together with those of {{jalink|Kuwoyama Shrine|Q11650775}}, opened a channel through Hozukyo to reclaim the former lake area of the {{jalink|Kameoka Basin|Q11181997}}. The site at the basin entrance was politically important, and the Hata clan is said to have advanced here early. Shrine tradition also links the old place-name "Tanba" to "Taniba."''Kameoka Shrine Gazetteer'', Kuwada Shrine section. The shrine buildings burned in the Tenmei era (1781-1789). Until 1873, it reportedly shared the name "Uketa Shrine (Ukita Shrine)" with the shrine across the river.On-site explanatory board. It was ranked village shrine in June 1873 and officially used the name "Kuwada Shrine" in June 1877. === Precinct === File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) honden.JPG|Honden File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) sandō.JPG|Approach File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) torii.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === * Jinushi Shrine * Gosha Shrine * Tenmangu * Suijingu * Kuzuryu Okami * Akiba-gu * Haraedo-gu === Access === * Nearest station: {{jalink|JR West|Q502125}} {{jalink|Sagano Line|Q1073011}} {{jalink|Umahori Station|Q1044103}} (about 13 minutes on foot) === Nearby === * {{jalink|Uketa Shrine|Q11631810}} === Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka) === {{Shrine | name = Kuwada Shrine | image = [[File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō umahori, Kameoka) keidai.JPG|250px]]
Honden in center, Tenmangu on right | location = {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}{{jalink|Kameoka|Q633996}} Shinocho Umahori Higashigaito 3 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|00|31.38|N|135|36|08.36|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|name=Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka)}} | deity = {{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} | status = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small), {{jalink|ronsha|Q135022904}}
former {{jalink|mukakusha}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20160304122202/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/tanba/bun/tab380102-02.html Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori Higashigaito, Kameoka)] (private research site). | founded = Unknown | annual_festival = {{jalink|October 25|Q2954}} | map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Kuwada Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|35|00|31.38|N|135|36|08.36|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|00|31.38|N|135|36|08.36|E}}}} }} '''Kuwada Shrine''' in Umahori is the second candidate shrine. Its former rank was mukakusha. === Enshrined deity === * {{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} === History === Its history is unclear. {{wikidata link|Q11537594}} == Japanese content == {{Coordinates list}} '''Kuwada Shrine''' (くわだじんじゃ / くわたじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} listed in the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}}'' for Kuwata District, {{jalink|Tanba Province|Q954511}}. The following two shrines are treated as candidate shrines ({{jalink|ronsha|Q135022904}}): * 1. {{jalink|Kuwada Shrine#Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto, Kameoka)|Q11537594|Kuwada Shrine}} (Shinocho Yamamoto, {{jalink|Kameoka|Q633996}}, {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}) - former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} * 2. {{jalink|Kuwada Shrine#Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka)|Q11537594|Kuwada Shrine}} (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka, Kyoto) - former {{jalink|mukakusha}} === Overview === The ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' records "Kuwada Shrine, Kuwata District, Tanba Province." Two candidate shrines survive in Kameoka City. The Umahori shrine is sometimes said to be the former original site of the Yamamoto shrine, but the exact history is unclear. Both are in eastern Kameoka near the entrance to {{jalink|Hozukyo Gorge|Q10887680}} on the right bank of the Oi River; {{jalink|Uketa Shrine|Q11631810}} stands across the river. === Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto, Kameoka) === {{Shrine | name = Kuwada Shrine | image = [[File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) keidai.JPG|280px]]
Haiden (left) and honden (rear right) | location = {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}{{jalink|Kameoka|Q633996}} Shinocho Yamamoto Kitajo 50.51 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|00|51.83|N|135|36|33.88|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|name=Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto, Kameoka)}} | deity = {{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} | status = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small), {{jalink|ronsha|Q135022904}}
former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} | founded = Unknown | architecture = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | annual_festival = {{jalink|October 25|Q2954}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20160304122457/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/tanba/bun/tab380102-01.html Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto Kitajo, Kameoka)] (private research site). | map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Kuwada Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|35|00|51.83|N|135|36|33.88|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|00|51.83|N|135|36|33.88|E}}}} }} '''Kuwada Shrine''' in Yamamoto is a Shinto shrine in Kameoka, Kyoto. It is a candidate Engishiki shrine; its former rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} ; Additional deities * {{jalink|Oyamakui|Q10935105|Oyamakui-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Oyamazumi|Q386563|Oyamazumi-no-Mikoto}} === History === Tradition says the deities, together with those of {{jalink|Kuwoyama Shrine|Q11650775}}, opened a channel through Hozukyo to reclaim the former lake area of the {{jalink|Kameoka Basin|Q11181997}}. The site at the basin entrance was politically important, and the Hata clan is said to have advanced here early. Shrine tradition also links the old place-name "Tanba" to "Taniba."''Kameoka Shrine Gazetteer'', Kuwada Shrine section. The shrine buildings burned in the Tenmei era (1781-1789). Until 1873, it reportedly shared the name "Uketa Shrine (Ukita Shrine)" with the shrine across the river.On-site explanatory board. It was ranked village shrine in June 1873 and officially used the name "Kuwada Shrine" in June 1877. === Precinct === File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) honden.JPG|Honden File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) sandō.JPG|Approach File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) torii.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === * Jinushi Shrine * Gosha Shrine * Tenmangu * Suijingu * Kuzuryu Okami * Akiba-gu * Haraedo-gu === Access === * Nearest station: {{jalink|JR West|Q502125}} {{jalink|Sagano Line|Q1073011}} {{jalink|Umahori Station|Q1044103}} (about 13 minutes on foot) === Nearby === * {{jalink|Uketa Shrine|Q11631810}} === Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka) === {{Shrine | name = Kuwada Shrine | image = [[File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō umahori, Kameoka) keidai.JPG|250px]]
Honden in center, Tenmangu on right | location = {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}{{jalink|Kameoka|Q633996}} Shinocho Umahori Higashigaito 3 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|00|31.38|N|135|36|08.36|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|name=Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka)}} | deity = {{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} | status = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small), {{jalink|ronsha|Q135022904}}
former {{jalink|mukakusha}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20160304122202/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/tanba/bun/tab380102-02.html Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori Higashigaito, Kameoka)] (private research site). | founded = Unknown | annual_festival = {{jalink|October 25|Q2954}} | map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Kuwada Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|35|00|31.38|N|135|36|08.36|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|00|31.38|N|135|36|08.36|E}}}} }} '''Kuwada Shrine''' in Umahori is the second candidate shrine. Its former rank was mukakusha. === Enshrined deity === * {{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} === History === Its history is unclear. A tradition says the deity was moved from this site to the Yamamoto site. === Precinct === File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō umahori, Kameoka) honden.JPG|Protective housing over honden File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō umahori, Kameoka) torii.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrine === * Tenmangu === Access === * Nearest station: {{jalink|JR West|Q502125}} {{jalink|Sagano Line|Q1073011}} {{jalink|Umahori Station|Q1044103}} (about 3 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === {{Commons|Category:Kuwada-jinja (Kameoka)}} * On-site explanatory board (Yamamoto shrine only) * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Kyoto-fu no Chimei'' (Heibonsha), Kuwada Shrine entry * Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (ed.), ''Shikinaisha Chosa Hokoku'' Vol. 18, Kuwada Shrine entry {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くわたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kuwada Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kuwada Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:丹波国の式内社]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:京都府の旧無格社]] [[Category:丹波湖伝説]] [[Category:亀岡市の建築物]] [[Category:need translation]]

Both are in eastern Kameoka near the entrance to {{jalink|Hozukyo Gorge|Q10887680}} on the right bank of the Oi River; {{jalink|Uketa Shrine|Q11631810}} stands across the river. === Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto, Kameoka) === {{Shrine | name = Kuwada Shrine | image = [[File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) keidai.JPG|280px]]
Haiden (left) and honden (rear right) | location = {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}{{jalink|Kameoka|Q633996}} Shinocho Yamamoto Kitajo 50.51 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|00|51.83|N|135|36|33.88|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|name=Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto, Kameoka)}} | deity = {{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} | status = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small), {{jalink|ronsha|Q135022904}}
former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} | founded = Unknown | architecture = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | annual_festival = {{jalink|October 25|Q2954}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20160304122457/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/tanba/bun/tab380102-01.html Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto Kitajo, Kameoka)] (private research site). | map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Kuwada Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|35|00|51.83|N|135|36|33.88|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|00|51.83|N|135|36|33.88|E}}}} }} '''Kuwada Shrine''' in Yamamoto is a Shinto shrine in Kameoka, Kyoto. It is a candidate Engishiki shrine; its former rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} ; Additional deities * {{jalink|Oyamakui|Q10935105|Oyamakui-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Oyamazumi|Q386563|Oyamazumi-no-Mikoto}} === History === Tradition says the deities, together with those of {{jalink|Kuwoyama Shrine|Q11650775}}, opened a channel through Hozukyo to reclaim the former lake area of the {{jalink|Kameoka Basin|Q11181997}}. The site at the basin entrance was politically important, and the Hata clan is said to have advanced here early. Shrine tradition also links the old place-name "Tanba" to "Taniba."''Kameoka Shrine Gazetteer'', Kuwada Shrine section. The shrine buildings burned in the Tenmei era (1781-1789). Until 1873, it reportedly shared the name "Uketa Shrine (Ukita Shrine)" with the shrine across the river.On-site explanatory board. It was ranked village shrine in June 1873 and officially used the name "Kuwada Shrine" in June 1877. === Precinct === File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) honden.JPG|Honden File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) sandō.JPG|Approach File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) torii.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === * Jinushi Shrine * Gosha Shrine * Tenmangu * Suijingu * Kuzuryu Okami * Akiba-gu * Haraedo-gu === Access === * Nearest station: {{jalink|JR West|Q502125}} {{jalink|Sagano Line|Q1073011}} {{jalink|Umahori Station|Q1044103}} (about 13 minutes on foot) === Nearby === * {{jalink|Uketa Shrine|Q11631810}} === Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka) === {{Shrine | name = Kuwada Shrine | image = [[File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō umahori, Kameoka) keidai.JPG|250px]]
Honden in center, Tenmangu on right | location = {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}{{jalink|Kameoka|Q633996}} Shinocho Umahori Higashigaito 3 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|00|31.38|N|135|36|08.36|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|name=Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka)}} | deity = {{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} | status = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small), {{jalink|ronsha|Q135022904}}
former {{jalink|mukakusha}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20160304122202/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/tanba/bun/tab380102-02.html Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori Higashigaito, Kameoka)] (private research site). | founded = Unknown | annual_festival = {{jalink|October 25|Q2954}} | map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Kuwada Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|35|00|31.38|N|135|36|08.36|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|00|31.38|N|135|36|08.36|E}}}} }} '''Kuwada Shrine''' in Umahori is the second candidate shrine. Its former rank was mukakusha. === Enshrined deity === * {{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} === History === Its history is unclear. A tradition says the deity was moved from this site to the Yamamoto site. === Precinct === File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō umahori, Kameoka) honden.JPG|Protective housing over honden File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō umahori, Kameoka) torii.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrine === * Tenmangu === Access === * Nearest station: {{jalink|JR West|Q502125}} {{jalink|Sagano Line|Q1073011}} {{jalink|Umahori Station|Q1044103}} (about 3 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === {{Commons|Category:Kuwada-jinja (Kameoka)}} * On-site explanatory board (Yamamoto shrine only) * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Kyoto-fu no Chimei'' (Heibonsha), Kuwada Shrine entry * Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (ed.), ''Shikinaisha Chosa Hokoku'' Vol. 18, Kuwada Shrine entry {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くわたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kuwada Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kuwada Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:丹波国の式内社]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:京都府の旧無格社]] [[Category:丹波湖伝説]] [[Category:亀岡市の建築物]] [[Category:need translation]]

Ollama and DeepSeek were too bad to actually use. see categorization since some of them are very questionable, especially if there was a massive decrease in page size or the {{jawiki| template is minimal or absent on the page

{{wikidata link|Q11537594}} == Japanese content == {{Coordinates list}} '''Kuwada Shrine''' (くわだじんじゃ / くわたじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} listed in the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}}'' for Kuwata District, {{jalink|Tanba Province|Q954511}}. The following two shrines are treated as candidate shrines ({{jalink|ronsha|Q135022904}}): * 1. {{jalink|Kuwada Shrine#Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto, Kameoka)|Q11537594|Kuwada Shrine}} (Shinocho Yamamoto, {{jalink|Kameoka|Q633996}}, {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}) - former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} * 2. {{jalink|Kuwada Shrine#Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka)|Q11537594|Kuwada Shrine}} (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka, Kyoto) - former {{jalink|mukakusha}} === Overview === The ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' records "Kuwada Shrine, Kuwata District, Tanba Province." Two candidate shrines survive in Kameoka City. The Umahori shrine is sometimes said to be the former original site of the Yamamoto shrine, but the exact history is unclear. Both are in eastern Kameoka near the entrance to {{jalink|Hozukyo Gorge|Q10887680}} on the right bank of the Oi River; {{jalink|Uketa Shrine|Q11631810}} stands across the river. === Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto, Kameoka) === {{Shrine | name = Kuwada Shrine | image = [[File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) keidai.JPG|280px]]

It is a candidate Engishiki shrine; its former rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} ; Additional deities * {{jalink|Oyamakui|Q10935105|Oyamakui-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Oyamazumi|Q386563|Oyamazumi-no-Mikoto}} === History === Tradition says the deities, together with those of {{jalink|Kuwoyama Shrine|Q11650775}}, opened a channel through Hozukyo to reclaim the former lake area of the {{jalink|Kameoka Basin|Q11181997}}. The site at the basin entrance was politically important, and the Hata clan is said to have advanced here early. Shrine tradition also links the old place-name "Tanba" to "Taniba."''Kameoka Shrine Gazetteer'', Kuwada Shrine section. The shrine buildings burned in the Tenmei era (1781-1789). Until 1873, it reportedly shared the name "Uketa Shrine (Ukita Shrine)" with the shrine across the river.On-site explanatory board. It was ranked village shrine in June 1873 and officially used the name "Kuwada Shrine" in June 1877. === Precinct === File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) honden.JPG|Honden File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) sandō.JPG|Approach File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) torii.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === * Jinushi Shrine * Gosha Shrine * Tenmangu * Suijingu * Kuzuryu Okami * Akiba-gu * Haraedo-gu === Access === * Nearest station: {{jalink|JR West|Q502125}} {{jalink|Sagano Line|Q1073011}} {{jalink|Umahori Station|Q1044103}} (about 13 minutes on foot) === Nearby === * {{jalink|Uketa Shrine|Q11631810}} === Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka) === {{Shrine | name = Kuwada Shrine | image = [[File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō umahori, Kameoka) keidai.JPG|250px]]

{{wikidata link|Q11537594}} == Japanese content == {{Coordinates list}} '''Kuwada Shrine''' (くわだじんじゃ / くわたじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} listed in the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}}'' for Kuwata District, {{jalink|Tanba Province|Q954511}}. The following two shrines are treated as candidate shrines ({{jalink|ronsha|Q135022904}}): * 1. {{jalink|Kuwada Shrine#Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto, Kameoka)|Q11537594|Kuwada Shrine}} (Shinocho Yamamoto, {{jalink|Kameoka|Q633996}}, {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}) - former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} * 2. {{jalink|Kuwada Shrine#Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka)|Q11537594|Kuwada Shrine}} (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka, Kyoto) - former {{jalink|mukakusha}} === Overview === The ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' records "Kuwada Shrine, Kuwata District, Tanba Province." Two candidate shrines survive in Kameoka City. The Umahori shrine is sometimes said to be the former original site of the Yamamoto shrine, but the exact history is unclear. Both are in eastern Kameoka near the entrance to {{jalink|Hozukyo Gorge|Q10887680}} on the right bank of the Oi River; {{jalink|Uketa Shrine|Q11631810}} stands across the river. === Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto, Kameoka) === {{Shrine | name = Kuwada Shrine | image = [[File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) keidai.JPG|280px]]
Haiden (left) and honden (rear right) | location = {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}{{jalink|Kameoka|Q633996}} Shinocho Yamamoto Kitajo 50.51 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|00|51.83|N|135|36|33.88|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|name=Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto, Kameoka)}} | deity = {{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} | status = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small), {{jalink|ronsha|Q135022904}}
former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} | founded = Unknown | architecture = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | annual_festival = {{jalink|October 25|Q2954}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20160304122457/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/tanba/bun/tab380102-01.html Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto Kitajo, Kameoka)] (private research site). | map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Kuwada Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|35|00|51.83|N|135|36|33.88|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|00|51.83|N|135|36|33.88|E}}}} }} '''Kuwada Shrine''' in Yamamoto is a Shinto shrine in Kameoka, Kyoto. It is a candidate Engishiki shrine; its former rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} ; Additional deities * {{jalink|Oyamakui|Q10935105|Oyamakui-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Oyamazumi|Q386563|Oyamazumi-no-Mikoto}} === History === Tradition says the deities, together with those of {{jalink|Kuwoyama Shrine|Q11650775}}, opened a channel through Hozukyo to reclaim the former lake area of the {{jalink|Kameoka Basin|Q11181997}}. The site at the basin entrance was politically important, and the Hata clan is said to have advanced here early. Shrine tradition also links the old place-name "Tanba" to "Taniba."''Kameoka Shrine Gazetteer'', Kuwada Shrine section. The shrine buildings burned in the Tenmei era (1781-1789). Until 1873, it reportedly shared the name "Uketa Shrine (Ukita Shrine)" with the shrine across the river.On-site explanatory board. It was ranked village shrine in June 1873 and officially used the name "Kuwada Shrine" in June 1877. === Precinct === File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) honden.JPG|Honden File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) sandō.JPG|Approach File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) torii.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === * Jinushi Shrine * Gosha Shrine * Tenmangu * Suijingu * Kuzuryu Okami * Akiba-gu * Haraedo-gu === Access === * Nearest station: {{jalink|JR West|Q502125}} {{jalink|Sagano Line|Q1073011}} {{jalink|Umahori Station|Q1044103}} (about 13 minutes on foot) === Nearby === * {{jalink|Uketa Shrine|Q11631810}} === Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka) === {{Shrine | name = Kuwada Shrine | image = [[File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō umahori, Kameoka) keidai.JPG|250px]]
Honden in center, Tenmangu on right | location = {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}{{jalink|Kameoka|Q633996}} Shinocho Umahori Higashigaito 3 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|00|31.38|N|135|36|08.36|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|name=Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka)}} | deity = {{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} | status = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small), {{jalink|ronsha|Q135022904}}
former {{jalink|mukakusha}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20160304122202/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/tanba/bun/tab380102-02.html Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori Higashigaito, Kameoka)] (private research site). | founded = Unknown | annual_festival = {{jalink|October 25|Q2954}} | map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Kuwada Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|35|00|31.38|N|135|36|08.36|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|00|31.38|N|135|36|08.36|E}}}} }} '''Kuwada Shrine''' in Umahori is the second candidate shrine. Its former rank was mukakusha. === Enshrined deity === * {{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} === History === Its history is unclear. A tradition says the deity was moved from this site to the Yamamoto site. === Precinct === File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō umahori, Kameoka) honden.JPG|Protective housing over honden File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō umahori, Kameoka) torii.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrine === * Tenmangu === Access === * Nearest station: {{jalink|JR West|Q502125}} {{jalink|Sagano Line|Q1073011}} {{jalink|Umahori Station|Q1044103}} (about 3 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === {{Commons|Category:Kuwada-jinja (Kameoka)}} * On-site explanatory board (Yamamoto shrine only) * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Kyoto-fu no Chimei'' (Heibonsha), Kuwada Shrine entry * Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (ed.), ''Shikinaisha Chosa Hokoku'' Vol. 18, Kuwada Shrine entry {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くわたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kuwada Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kuwada Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:丹波国の式内社]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:京都府の旧無格社]] [[Category:丹波湖伝説]] [[Category:亀岡市の建築物]] [[Category:need translation]]

{{wikidata link|Q11537594}} == Japanese content == {{Coordinates list}} '''Kuwada Shrine''' (くわだじんじゃ / くわたじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} listed in the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}}'' for Kuwata District, {{jalink|Tanba Province|Q954511}}. The following two shrines are treated as candidate shrines ({{jalink|ronsha|Q135022904}}): * 1. {{jalink|Kuwada Shrine#Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto, Kameoka)|Q11537594|Kuwada Shrine}} (Shinocho Yamamoto, {{jalink|Kameoka|Q633996}}, {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}) - former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} * 2. {{jalink|Kuwada Shrine#Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka)|Q11537594|Kuwada Shrine}} (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka, Kyoto) - former {{jalink|mukakusha}} === Overview === The ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' records "Kuwada Shrine, Kuwata District, Tanba Province." Two candidate shrines survive in Kameoka City. The Umahori shrine is sometimes said to be the former original site of the Yamamoto shrine, but the exact history is unclear. Both are in eastern Kameoka near the entrance to {{jalink|Hozukyo Gorge|Q10887680}} on the right bank of the Oi River; {{jalink|Uketa Shrine|Q11631810}} stands across the river. === Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto, Kameoka) === {{Shrine | name = Kuwada Shrine | image = [[File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) keidai.JPG|280px]]
Haiden (left) and honden (rear right) | location = {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}{{jalink|Kameoka|Q633996}} Shinocho Yamamoto Kitajo 50.51 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|00|51.83|N|135|36|33.88|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|name=Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto, Kameoka)}} | deity = {{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} | status = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small), {{jalink|ronsha|Q135022904}}
former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} | founded = Unknown | architecture = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | annual_festival = {{jalink|October 25|Q2954}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20160304122457/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/tanba/bun/tab380102-01.html Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto Kitajo, Kameoka)] (private research site). | map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Kuwada Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|35|00|51.83|N|135|36|33.88|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|00|51.83|N|135|36|33.88|E}}}} }} '''Kuwada Shrine''' in Yamamoto is a Shinto shrine in Kameoka, Kyoto. It is a candidate Engishiki shrine; its former rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} ; Additional deities * {{jalink|Oyamakui|Q10935105|Oyamakui-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Oyamazumi|Q386563|Oyamazumi-no-Mikoto}} === History === Tradition says the deities, together with those of {{jalink|Kuwoyama Shrine|Q11650775}}, opened a channel through Hozukyo to reclaim the former lake area of the {{jalink|Kameoka Basin|Q11181997}}. The site at the basin entrance was politically important, and the Hata clan is said to have advanced here early. Shrine tradition also links the old place-name "Tanba" to "Taniba."''Kameoka Shrine Gazetteer'', Kuwada Shrine section. The shrine buildings burned in the Tenmei era (1781-1789). Until 1873, it reportedly shared the name "Uketa Shrine (Ukita Shrine)" with the shrine across the river.On-site explanatory board. It was ranked village shrine in June 1873 and officially used the name "Kuwada Shrine" in June 1877. === Precinct === File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) honden.JPG|Honden File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) sandō.JPG|Approach File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) torii.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === * Jinushi Shrine * Gosha Shrine * Tenmangu * Suijingu * Kuzuryu Okami * Akiba-gu * Haraedo-gu === Access === * Nearest station: {{jalink|JR West|Q502125}} {{jalink|Sagano Line|Q1073011}} {{jalink|Umahori Station|Q1044103}} (about 13 minutes on foot) === Nearby === * {{jalink|Uketa Shrine|Q11631810}} === Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka) === {{Shrine | name = Kuwada Shrine | image = [[File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō umahori, Kameoka) keidai.JPG|250px]]
Honden in center, Tenmangu on right | location = {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}{{jalink|Kameoka|Q633996}} Shinocho Umahori Higashigaito 3 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|00|31.38|N|135|36|08.36|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|name=Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka)}} | deity = {{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} | status = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small), {{jalink|ronsha|Q135022904}}
former {{jalink|mukakusha}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20160304122202/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/tanba/bun/tab380102-02.html Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori Higashigaito, Kameoka)] (private research site). | founded = Unknown | annual_festival = {{jalink|October 25|Q2954}} | map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Kuwada Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|35|00|31.38|N|135|36|08.36|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|00|31.38|N|135|36|08.36|E}}}} }} '''Kuwada Shrine''' in Umahori is the second candidate shrine. Its former rank was mukakusha. === Enshrined deity === * {{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} === History === Its history is unclear. A tradition says the deity was moved from this site to the Yamamoto site. === Precinct === File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō umahori, Kameoka) honden.JPG|Protective housing over honden File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō umahori, Kameoka) torii.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrine === * Tenmangu === Access === * Nearest station: {{jalink|JR West|Q502125}} {{jalink|Sagano Line|Q1073011}} {{jalink|Umahori Station|Q1044103}} (about 3 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === {{Commons|Category:Kuwada-jinja (Kameoka)}} * On-site explanatory board (Yamamoto shrine only) * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Kyoto-fu no Chimei'' (Heibonsha), Kuwada Shrine entry * Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (ed.), ''Shikinaisha Chosa Hokoku'' Vol. 18, Kuwada Shrine entry {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くわたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kuwada Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kuwada Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:丹波国の式内社]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:京都府の旧無格社]] [[Category:丹波湖伝説]] [[Category:亀岡市の建築物]]

for Kuwata District, {{jalink|Tanba Province|Q954511}}. The following two shrines are treated as candidate shrines ({{jalink|ronsha|Q135022904}}): * 1. {{jalink|Kuwada Shrine#Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto, Kameoka)|Q11537594|Kuwada Shrine}} (Shinocho Yamamoto, {{jalink|Kameoka|Q633996}}, {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}) - former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} * 2. {{jalink|Kuwada Shrine#Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka)|Q11537594|Kuwada Shrine}} (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka, Kyoto) - former {{jalink|mukakusha}} === Overview === The ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' records "Kuwada Shrine, Kuwata District, Tanba Province." Two candidate shrines survive in Kameoka City. The Umahori shrine is sometimes said to be the former original site of the Yamamoto shrine, but the exact history is unclear. Both are in eastern Kameoka near the entrance to {{jalink|Hozukyo Gorge|Q10887680}} on the right bank of the Oi River; {{jalink|Uketa Shrine|Q11631810}} stands across the river. === Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto, Kameoka) === {{Shrine | name = Kuwada Shrine | image = [[File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) keidai.JPG|280px]]
Haiden (left) and honden (rear right) | location = {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}{{jalink|Kameoka|Q633996}} Shinocho Yamamoto Kitajo 50.51 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|00|51.83|N|135|36|33.88|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|name=Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto, Kameoka)}} | deity = {{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} | status = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small), {{jalink|ronsha|Q135022904}}
former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} | founded = Unknown | architecture = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | annual_festival = {{jalink|October 25|Q2954}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20160304122457/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/tanba/bun/tab380102-01.html Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto Kitajo, Kameoka)] (private research site). | map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Kuwada Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|35|00|51.83|N|135|36|33.88|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|00|51.83|N|135|36|33.88|E}}}} }} '''Kuwada Shrine''' in Yamamoto is a Shinto shrine in Kameoka, Kyoto. It is a candidate Engishiki shrine; its former rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} ; Additional deities * {{jalink|Oyamakui|Q10935105|Oyamakui-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Oyamazumi|Q386563|Oyamazumi-no-Mikoto}} === History === Tradition says the deities, together with those of {{jalink|Kuwoyama Shrine|Q11650775}}, opened a channel through Hozukyo to reclaim the former lake area of the {{jalink|Kameoka Basin|Q11181997}}. The site at the basin entrance was politically important, and the Hata clan is said to have advanced here early. Shrine tradition also links the old place-name "Tanba" to "Taniba."''Kameoka Shrine Gazetteer'', Kuwada Shrine section. The shrine buildings burned in the Tenmei era (1781-1789). Until 1873, it reportedly shared the name "Uketa Shrine (Ukita Shrine)" with the shrine across the river.On-site explanatory board. It was ranked village shrine in June 1873 and officially used the name "Kuwada Shrine" in June 1877. === Precinct === File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) honden.JPG|Honden File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) sandō.JPG|Approach File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) torii.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === * Jinushi Shrine * Gosha Shrine * Tenmangu * Suijingu * Kuzuryu Okami * Akiba-gu * Haraedo-gu === Access === * Nearest station: {{jalink|JR West|Q502125}} {{jalink|Sagano Line|Q1073011}} {{jalink|Umahori Station|Q1044103}} (about 13 minutes on foot) === Nearby === * {{jalink|Uketa Shrine|Q11631810}} === Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka) === {{Shrine | name = Kuwada Shrine | image = [[File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō umahori, Kameoka) keidai.JPG|250px]]
Honden in center, Tenmangu on right | location = {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}{{jalink|Kameoka|Q633996}} Shinocho Umahori Higashigaito 3 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|00|31.38|N|135|36|08.36|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|name=Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka)}} | deity = {{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} | status = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small), {{jalink|ronsha|Q135022904}}
former {{jalink|mukakusha}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20160304122202/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/tanba/bun/tab380102-02.html Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori Higashigaito, Kameoka)] (private research site). | founded = Unknown | annual_festival = {{jalink|October 25|Q2954}} | map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Kuwada Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|35|00|31.38|N|135|36|08.36|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|00|31.38|N|135|36|08.36|E}}}} }} '''Kuwada Shrine''' in Umahori is the second candidate shrine. Its former rank was mukakusha. === Enshrined deity === * {{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} === History === Its history is unclear. A tradition says the deity was moved from this site to the Yamamoto site. === Precinct === File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō umahori, Kameoka) honden.JPG|Protective housing over honden File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō umahori, Kameoka) torii.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrine === * Tenmangu === Access === * Nearest station: {{jalink|JR West|Q502125}} {{jalink|Sagano Line|Q1073011}} {{jalink|Umahori Station|Q1044103}} (about 3 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === {{Commons|Category:Kuwada-jinja (Kameoka)}} * On-site explanatory board (Yamamoto shrine only) * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Kyoto-fu no Chimei'' (Heibonsha), Kuwada Shrine entry * Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (ed.), ''Shikinaisha Chosa Hokoku'' Vol. 18, Kuwada Shrine entry {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くわたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kuwada Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kuwada Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:丹波国の式内社]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:京都府の旧無格社]] [[Category:丹波湖伝説]] [[Category:亀岡市の建築物]][[Category:qq]]

{{wikidata link|Q11537594}} == Japanese content == {{Coordinates list}} '''Kuwada Shrine''' (くわだじんじゃ / くわたじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} listed in the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}}'' for Kuwata District, {{jalink|Tanba Province|Q954511}}. The following two shrines are treated as candidate shrines ({{jalink|ronsha|Q135022904}}): * 1. {{jalink|Kuwada Shrine#Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto, Kameoka)|Q11537594|Kuwada Shrine}} (Shinocho Yamamoto, {{jalink|Kameoka|Q633996}}, {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}) - former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} * 2. {{jalink|Kuwada Shrine#Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka)|Q11537594|Kuwada Shrine}} (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka, Kyoto) - former {{jalink|mukakusha}} === Overview === The ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' records "Kuwada Shrine, Kuwata District, Tanba Province." Two candidate shrines survive in Kameoka City. The Umahori shrine is sometimes said to be the former original site of the Yamamoto shrine, but the exact history is unclear. Both are in eastern Kameoka near the entrance to {{jalink|Hozukyo Gorge|Q10887680}} on the right bank of the Oi River; {{jalink|Uketa Shrine|Q11631810}} stands across the river. === Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto, Kameoka) === {{Shrine | name = Kuwada Shrine | image = [[File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) keidai.JPG|280px]]

former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} | founded = Unknown | architecture = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | annual_festival = {{jalink|October 25|Q2954}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20160304122457/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/tanba/bun/tab380102-01.html Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto Kitajo, Kameoka)] (private research site). | map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Kuwada Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|35|00|51.83|N|135|36|33.88|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|00|51.83|N|135|36|33.88|E}}}} }} '''Kuwada Shrine''' in Yamamoto is a Shinto shrine in Kameoka, Kyoto. It is a candidate Engishiki shrine; its former rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} ; Additional deities * {{jalink|Oyamakui|Q10935105|Oyamakui-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Oyamazumi|Q386563|Oyamazumi-no-Mikoto}} === History === Tradition says the deities, together with those of {{jalink|Kuwoyama Shrine|Q11650775}}, opened a channel through Hozukyo to reclaim the former lake area of the {{jalink|Kameoka Basin|Q11181997}}. The site at the basin entrance was politically important, and the Hata clan is said to have advanced here early. Shrine tradition also links the old place-name "Tanba" to "Taniba."''Kameoka Shrine Gazetteer'', Kuwada Shrine section. The shrine buildings burned in the Tenmei era (1781-1789). Until 1873, it reportedly shared the name "Uketa Shrine (Ukita Shrine)" with the shrine across the river.On-site explanatory board. It was ranked village shrine in June 1873 and officially used the name "Kuwada Shrine" in June 1877. === Precinct === File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) honden.JPG|Honden File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) sandō.JPG|Approach File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) torii.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === * Jinushi Shrine * Gosha Shrine * Tenmangu * Suijingu * Kuzuryu Okami * Akiba-gu * Haraedo-gu === Access === * Nearest station: {{jalink|JR West|Q502125}} {{jalink|Sagano Line|Q1073011}} {{jalink|Umahori Station|Q1044103}} (about 13 minutes on foot) === Nearby === * {{jalink|Uketa Shrine|Q11631810}} === Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka) === {{Shrine | name = Kuwada Shrine | image = [[File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō umahori, Kameoka) keidai.JPG|250px]]

{{wikidata link|Q11537594}} == Japanese content == {{Coordinates list}} '''Kuwada Shrine''' (くわだじんじゃ / くわたじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} listed in the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}}'' for Kuwata District, {{jalink|Tanba Province|Q954511}}. The following two shrines are treated as candidate shrines ({{jalink|ronsha|Q135022904}}): * 1. {{jalink|Kuwada Shrine#Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto, Kameoka)|Q11537594|Kuwada Shrine}} (Shinocho Yamamoto, {{jalink|Kameoka|Q633996}}, {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}) - former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} * 2. {{jalink|Kuwada Shrine#Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka)|Q11537594|Kuwada Shrine}} (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka, Kyoto) - former {{jalink|mukakusha}} === Overview === The ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' records "Kuwada Shrine, Kuwata District, Tanba Province." Two candidate shrines survive in Kameoka City. The Umahori shrine is sometimes said to be the former original site of the Yamamoto shrine, but the exact history is unclear. Both are in eastern Kameoka near the entrance to {{jalink|Hozukyo Gorge|Q10887680}} on the right bank of the Oi River; {{jalink|Uketa Shrine|Q11631810}} stands across the river. === Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto, Kameoka) === {{Shrine | name = Kuwada Shrine | image = [[File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) keidai.JPG|280px]]
Haiden (left) and honden (rear right) | location = {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}{{jalink|Kameoka|Q633996}} Shinocho Yamamoto Kitajo 50.51 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|00|51.83|N|135|36|33.88|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|name=Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto, Kameoka)}} | deity = {{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} | status = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small), {{jalink|ronsha|Q135022904}}
former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} | founded = Unknown | architecture = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | annual_festival = {{jalink|October 25|Q2954}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20160304122457/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/tanba/bun/tab380102-01.html Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto Kitajo, Kameoka)] (private research site). | map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Kuwada Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|35|00|51.83|N|135|36|33.88|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|00|51.83|N|135|36|33.88|E}}}} }} '''Kuwada Shrine''' in Yamamoto is a Shinto shrine in Kameoka, Kyoto. It is a candidate Engishiki shrine; its former rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} ; Additional deities * {{jalink|Oyamakui|Q10935105|Oyamakui-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Oyamazumi|Q386563|Oyamazumi-no-Mikoto}} === History === Tradition says the deities, together with those of {{jalink|Kuwoyama Shrine|Q11650775}}, opened a channel through Hozukyo to reclaim the former lake area of the {{jalink|Kameoka Basin|Q11181997}}. The site at the basin entrance was politically important, and the Hata clan is said to have advanced here early. Shrine tradition also links the old place-name "Tanba" to "Taniba."''Kameoka Shrine Gazetteer'', Kuwada Shrine section. The shrine buildings burned in the Tenmei era (1781-1789). Until 1873, it reportedly shared the name "Uketa Shrine (Ukita Shrine)" with the shrine across the river.On-site explanatory board. It was ranked village shrine in June 1873 and officially used the name "Kuwada Shrine" in June 1877. === Precinct === File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) honden.JPG|Honden File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) sandō.JPG|Approach File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) torii.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === * Jinushi Shrine * Gosha Shrine * Tenmangu * Suijingu * Kuzuryu Okami * Akiba-gu * Haraedo-gu === Access === * Nearest station: {{jalink|JR West|Q502125}} {{jalink|Sagano Line|Q1073011}} {{jalink|Umahori Station|Q1044103}} (about 13 minutes on foot) === Nearby === * {{jalink|Uketa Shrine|Q11631810}} === Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka) === {{Shrine | name = Kuwada Shrine | image = [[File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō umahori, Kameoka) keidai.JPG|250px]]
Honden in center, Tenmangu on right | location = {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}{{jalink|Kameoka|Q633996}} Shinocho Umahori Higashigaito 3 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|00|31.38|N|135|36|08.36|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|name=Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka)}} | deity = {{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} | status = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small), {{jalink|ronsha|Q135022904}}
former {{jalink|mukakusha}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20160304122202/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/tanba/bun/tab380102-02.html Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori Higashigaito, Kameoka)] (private research site). | founded = Unknown | annual_festival = {{jalink|October 25|Q2954}} | map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Kuwada Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|35|00|31.38|N|135|36|08.36|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|00|31.38|N|135|36|08.36|E}}}} }} '''Kuwada Shrine''' in Umahori is the second candidate shrine. Its former rank was mukakusha. === Enshrined deity === * {{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} === History === Its history is unclear. A tradition says the deity was moved from this site to the Yamamoto site. === Precinct === File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō umahori, Kameoka) honden.JPG|Protective housing over honden File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō umahori, Kameoka) torii.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrine === * Tenmangu === Access === * Nearest station: {{jalink|JR West|Q502125}} {{jalink|Sagano Line|Q1073011}} {{jalink|Umahori Station|Q1044103}} (about 3 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === {{Commons|Category:Kuwada-jinja (Kameoka)}} * On-site explanatory board (Yamamoto shrine only) * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Kyoto-fu no Chimei'' (Heibonsha), Kuwada Shrine entry * Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (ed.), ''Shikinaisha Chosa Hokoku'' Vol. 18, Kuwada Shrine entry {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くわたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kuwada Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kuwada Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:丹波国の式内社]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:京都府の旧無格社]] [[Category:丹波湖伝説]] [[Category:亀岡市の建築物]][[Category:qqq]]

A tradition says the deity was moved from this site to the Yamamoto site. === Precinct === File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō umahori, Kameoka) honden.JPG|Protective housing over honden File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō umahori, Kameoka) torii.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrine === * Tenmangu === Access === * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125}} {{ill|lt=Sagano Line|Q1073011|WD=Q1073011}} {{ill|lt=Umahori Station|Q1044103|WD=Q1044103}} (about 3 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === {{Commons|Category:Kuwada-jinja (Kameoka)}} * On-site explanatory board (Yamamoto shrine only) * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Kyoto-fu no Chimei'' (Heibonsha), Kuwada Shrine entry * Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (ed.), ''Shikinaisha Chosa Hokoku'' Vol. 18, Kuwada Shrine entry {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くわたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kuwada Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kuwada Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:丹波国の式内社]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:京都府の旧無格社]] [[Category:丹波湖伝説]] [[Category:亀岡市の建築物]][[Category:qqq]]

{{wikidata link|Q11537594}} == Japanese content == {{Coordinates list}} '''Kuwada Shrine''' (くわだじんじゃ / くわたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} listed in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}'' for Kuwata District, {{ill|lt=Tanba Province|Q954511|WD=Q954511}}. The following two shrines are treated as candidate shrines ({{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}}): * 1. {{ill|lt=Kuwada Shrine#Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto, Kameoka)|Q11537594|WD=Q11537594|lt=Kuwada Shrine}} (Shinocho Yamamoto, {{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}) - former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} * 2. {{ill|lt=Kuwada Shrine#Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka)|Q11537594|WD=Q11537594|lt=Kuwada Shrine}} (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka, Kyoto) - former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=mukakusha|UNKNOWN}} === Overview === The ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' records "Kuwada Shrine, Kuwata District, Tanba Province." Two candidate shrines survive in Kameoka City. The Umahori shrine is sometimes said to be the former original site of the Yamamoto shrine, but the exact history is unclear. Both are in eastern Kameoka near the entrance to {{ill|lt=Hozukyo Gorge|Q10887680|WD=Q10887680}} on the right bank of the Oi River; {{ill|lt=Uketa Shrine|Q11631810|WD=Q11631810}} stands across the river. === Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto, Kameoka) === {{Shrine | name = Kuwada Shrine | image = [[File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) keidai.JPG|280px]]
Haiden (left) and honden (rear right) | location = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996}} Shinocho Yamamoto Kitajo 50.51 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|00|51.83|N|135|36|33.88|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|name=Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto, Kameoka)}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (small), {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}}
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} | founded = Unknown | architecture = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | annual_festival = {{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|WD=Q2954}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20160304122457/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/tanba/bun/tab380102-01.html Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto Kitajo, Kameoka)] (private research site). | map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Kuwada Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|35|00|51.83|N|135|36|33.88|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|00|51.83|N|135|36|33.88|E}}}} }} '''Kuwada Shrine''' in Yamamoto is a Shinto shrine in Kameoka, Kyoto. It is a candidate Engishiki shrine; its former rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} ; Additional deities * {{ill|lt=Oyamakui|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|lt=Oyamakui-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Oyamazumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Oyamazumi-no-Mikoto}} === History === Tradition says the deities, together with those of {{ill|lt=Kuwoyama Shrine|Q11650775|WD=Q11650775}}, opened a channel through Hozukyo to reclaim the former lake area of the {{ill|lt=Kameoka Basin|Q11181997|WD=Q11181997}}. The site at the basin entrance was politically important, and the Hata clan is said to have advanced here early. Shrine tradition also links the old place-name "Tanba" to "Taniba."''Kameoka Shrine Gazetteer'', Kuwada Shrine section. The shrine buildings burned in the Tenmei era (1781-1789). Until 1873, it reportedly shared the name "Uketa Shrine (Ukita Shrine)" with the shrine across the river.On-site explanatory board. It was ranked village shrine in June 1873 and officially used the name "Kuwada Shrine" in June 1877. === Precinct === File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) honden.JPG|Honden File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) sandō.JPG|Approach File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) torii.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === * Jinushi Shrine * Gosha Shrine * Tenmangu * Suijingu * Kuzuryu Okami * Akiba-gu * Haraedo-gu === Access === * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125}} {{ill|lt=Sagano Line|Q1073011|WD=Q1073011}} {{ill|lt=Umahori Station|Q1044103|WD=Q1044103}} (about 13 minutes on foot) === Nearby === * {{ill|lt=Uketa Shrine|Q11631810|WD=Q11631810}} === Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka) === {{Shrine | name = Kuwada Shrine | image = [[File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō umahori, Kameoka) keidai.JPG|250px]]
Honden in center, Tenmangu on right | location = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996}} Shinocho Umahori Higashigaito 3 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|00|31.38|N|135|36|08.36|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|name=Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka)}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (small), {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=mukakusha|UNKNOWN}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20160304122202/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/tanba/bun/tab380102-02.html Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori Higashigaito, Kameoka)] (private research site). | founded = Unknown | annual_festival = {{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|WD=Q2954}} | map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Kuwada Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|35|00|31.38|N|135|36|08.36|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|00|31.38|N|135|36|08.36|E}}}} }} '''Kuwada Shrine''' in Umahori is the second candidate shrine. Its former rank was mukakusha. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} === History === Its history is unclear. A tradition says the deity was moved from this site to the Yamamoto site. === Precinct === File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō umahori, Kameoka) honden.JPG|Protective housing over honden File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō umahori, Kameoka) torii.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrine === * Tenmangu === Access === * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125}} {{ill|lt=Sagano Line|Q1073011|WD=Q1073011}} {{ill|lt=Umahori Station|Q1044103|WD=Q1044103}} (about 3 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === {{Commons|Category:Kuwada-jinja (Kameoka)}} * On-site explanatory board (Yamamoto shrine only) * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Kyoto-fu no Chimei'' (Heibonsha), Kuwada Shrine entry * Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (ed.), ''Shikinaisha Chosa Hokoku'' Vol. 18, Kuwada Shrine entry {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くわたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kuwada Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kuwada Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:丹波国の式内社]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:京都府の旧無格社]] [[Category:丹波湖伝説]] [[Category:亀岡市の建築物]][[Category:qqq]]

Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}'' for Kuwata District, {{ill|lt=Tanba Province|Q954511|WD=Q954511}}. The following two shrines are treated as candidate shrines ({{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}}): * 1. {{ill|lt=Kuwada Shrine#Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto, Kameoka)|Q11537594|WD=Q11537594|lt=Kuwada Shrine}} (Shinocho Yamamoto, {{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}) - former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} * 2. {{ill|lt=Kuwada Shrine#Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka)|Q11537594|WD=Q11537594|lt=Kuwada Shrine}} (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka, Kyoto) - former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=mukakusha|1=UNKNOWN}} === Overview === The ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' records "Kuwada Shrine, Kuwata District, Tanba Province." Two candidate shrines survive in Kameoka City. The Umahori shrine is sometimes said to be the former original site of the Yamamoto shrine, but the exact history is unclear. Both are in eastern Kameoka near the entrance to {{ill|lt=Hozukyo Gorge|Q10887680|WD=Q10887680}} on the right bank of the Oi River; {{ill|lt=Uketa Shrine|Q11631810|WD=Q11631810}} stands across the river. === Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto, Kameoka) === {{Shrine | name = Kuwada Shrine | image = [[File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) keidai.JPG|280px]]
Haiden (left) and honden (rear right) | location = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996}} Shinocho Yamamoto Kitajo 50.51 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|00|51.83|N|135|36|33.88|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|name=Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto, Kameoka)}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small), {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}}
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} | founded = Unknown | architecture = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | annual_festival = {{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|WD=Q2954}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20160304122457/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/tanba/bun/tab380102-01.html Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto Kitajo, Kameoka)] (private research site). | map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Kuwada Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|35|00|51.83|N|135|36|33.88|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|00|51.83|N|135|36|33.88|E}}}} }} '''Kuwada Shrine''' in Yamamoto is a Shinto shrine in Kameoka, Kyoto. It is a candidate Engishiki shrine; its former rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} ; Additional deities * {{ill|lt=Oyamakui|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|lt=Oyamakui-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Oyamazumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Oyamazumi-no-Mikoto}} === History === Tradition says the deities, together with those of {{ill|lt=Kuwoyama Shrine|Q11650775|WD=Q11650775}}, opened a channel through Hozukyo to reclaim the former lake area of the {{ill|lt=Kameoka Basin|Q11181997|WD=Q11181997}}. The site at the basin entrance was politically important, and the Hata clan is said to have advanced here early. Shrine tradition also links the old place-name "Tanba" to "Taniba."''Kameoka Shrine Gazetteer'', Kuwada Shrine section. The shrine buildings burned in the Tenmei era (1781-1789). Until 1873, it reportedly shared the name "Uketa Shrine (Ukita Shrine)" with the shrine across the river.On-site explanatory board. It was ranked village shrine in June 1873 and officially used the name "Kuwada Shrine" in June 1877. === Precinct === File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) honden.JPG|Honden File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) sandō.JPG|Approach File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) torii.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === * Jinushi Shrine * Gosha Shrine * Tenmangu * Suijingu * Kuzuryu Okami * Akiba-gu * Haraedo-gu === Access === * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125}} {{ill|lt=Sagano Line|Q1073011|WD=Q1073011}} {{ill|lt=Umahori Station|Q1044103|WD=Q1044103}} (about 13 minutes on foot) === Nearby === * {{ill|lt=Uketa Shrine|Q11631810|WD=Q11631810}} === Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka) === {{Shrine | name = Kuwada Shrine | image = [[File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō umahori, Kameoka) keidai.JPG|250px]]
Honden in center, Tenmangu on right | location = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996}} Shinocho Umahori Higashigaito 3 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|00|31.38|N|135|36|08.36|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|name=Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka)}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small), {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=mukakusha|1=UNKNOWN}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20160304122202/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/tanba/bun/tab380102-02.html Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori Higashigaito, Kameoka)] (private research site). | founded = Unknown | annual_festival = {{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|WD=Q2954}} | map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Kuwada Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|35|00|31.38|N|135|36|08.36|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|00|31.38|N|135|36|08.36|E}}}} }} '''Kuwada Shrine''' in Umahori is the second candidate shrine. Its former rank was mukakusha. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} === History === Its history is unclear. A tradition says the deity was moved from this site to the Yamamoto site. === Precinct === File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō umahori, Kameoka) honden.JPG|Protective housing over honden File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō umahori, Kameoka) torii.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrine === * Tenmangu === Access === * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125}} {{ill|lt=Sagano Line|Q1073011|WD=Q1073011}} {{ill|lt=Umahori Station|Q1044103|WD=Q1044103}} (about 3 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === {{Commons|Category:Kuwada-jinja (Kameoka)}} * On-site explanatory board (Yamamoto shrine only) * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Kyoto-fu no Chimei'' (Heibonsha), Kuwada Shrine entry * Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (ed.), ''Shikinaisha Chosa Hokoku'' Vol. 18, Kuwada Shrine entry {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くわたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kuwada Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kuwada Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:丹波国の式内社]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:京都府の旧無格社]] [[Category:丹波湖伝説]] [[Category:亀岡市の建築物]][[Category:qqq]]
