{{ill|Shikinai Ronsha|lt=Shikinai Ronsha|ja|論社|WD=Q135022904}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '京都府亀岡市篠町山本', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kuwada-jinja (Kameoka)}} [[JA:桑田神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11537594}} == Japanese content == {{Coordinates list}} '''Kuwada Shrine''' (くわだじんじゃ / くわたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} listed in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}'' for Kuwata District, {{ill|lt=Tanba Province|Q954511|WD=Q954511}}. The following two shrines are treated as candidate shrines ({{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}}): * 1. {{ill|lt=Kuwada Shrine#Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto, Kameoka)|Q11537594|WD=Q11537594|lt=Kuwada Shrine}} (Shinocho Yamamoto, {{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}) - former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} * 2. {{ill|lt=Kuwada Shrine#Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka)|Q11537594|WD=Q11537594|lt=Kuwada Shrine}} (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka, Kyoto) - former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=mukakusha|1=UNKNOWN}} === Overview === The ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' records "Kuwada Shrine, Kuwata District, Tanba Province." Two candidate shrines survive in Kameoka City. The Umahori shrine is sometimes said to be the former original site of the Yamamoto shrine, but the exact history is unclear. Both are in eastern Kameoka near the entrance to {{ill|lt=Hozukyo Gorge|Q10887680|WD=Q10887680}} on the right bank of the Oi River; {{ill|lt=Uketa Shrine|Q11631810|WD=Q11631810}} stands across the river. === Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto, Kameoka) === {{Shrine | name = Kuwada Shrine | image = [[File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) keidai.JPG|280px]]
Haiden (left) and honden (rear right) | location = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996}} Shinocho Yamamoto Kitajo 50.51 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|00|51.83|N|135|36|33.88|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|name=Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto, Kameoka)}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small), {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}}
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} | founded = Unknown | architecture = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | annual_festival = {{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|WD=Q2954}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20160304122457/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/tanba/bun/tab380102-01.html Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto Kitajo, Kameoka)] (private research site). | map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Kuwada Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|35|00|51.83|N|135|36|33.88|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|00|51.83|N|135|36|33.88|E}}}} }} '''Kuwada Shrine''' in Yamamoto is a Shinto shrine in Kameoka, Kyoto. It is a candidate Engishiki shrine; its former rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} ; Additional deities * {{ill|lt=Oyamakui|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|lt=Oyamakui-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Oyamazumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Oyamazumi-no-Mikoto}} === History === Tradition says the deities, together with those of {{ill|lt=Kuwoyama Shrine|Q11650775|WD=Q11650775}}, opened a channel through Hozukyo to reclaim the former lake area of the {{ill|lt=Kameoka Basin|Q11181997|WD=Q11181997}}. The site at the basin entrance was politically important, and the Hata clan is said to have advanced here early. Shrine tradition also links the old place-name "Tanba" to "Taniba."''Kameoka Shrine Gazetteer'', Kuwada Shrine section. The shrine buildings burned in the Tenmei era (1781-1789). Until 1873, it reportedly shared the name "Uketa Shrine (Ukita Shrine)" with the shrine across the river.On-site explanatory board. It was ranked village shrine in June 1873 and officially used the name "Kuwada Shrine" in June 1877. === Precinct === File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) honden.JPG|Honden File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) sandō.JPG|Approach File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) torii.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === * Jinushi Shrine * Gosha Shrine * Tenmangu * Suijingu * Kuzuryu Okami * Akiba-gu * Haraedo-gu === Access === * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125}} {{ill|lt=Sagano Line|Q1073011|WD=Q1073011}} {{ill|lt=Umahori Station|Q1044103|WD=Q1044103}} (about 13 minutes on foot) === Nearby === * {{ill|lt=Uketa Shrine|Q11631810|WD=Q11631810}} === Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka) === {{Shrine | name = Kuwada Shrine | image = [[File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō umahori, Kameoka) keidai.JPG|250px]]
Honden in center, Tenmangu on right | location = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996}} Shinocho Umahori Higashigaito 3 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|00|31.38|N|135|36|08.36|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|name=Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka)}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small), {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=mukakusha|1=UNKNOWN}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20160304122202/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/tanba/bun/tab380102-02.html Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori Higashigaito, Kameoka)] (private research site). | founded = Unknown | annual_festival = {{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|WD=Q2954}} | map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Kuwada Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|35|00|31.38|N|135|36|08.36|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|00|31.38|N|135|36|08.36|E}}}} }} '''Kuwada Shrine''' in Umahori is the second candidate shrine. Its former rank was mukakusha. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} === History === Its history is unclear. A tradition says the deity was moved from this site to the Yamamoto site. === Precinct === File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō umahori, Kameoka) honden.JPG|Protective housing over honden File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō umahori, Kameoka) torii.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrine === * Tenmangu === Access === * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125}} {{ill|lt=Sagano Line|Q1073011|WD=Q1073011}} {{ill|lt=Umahori Station|Q1044103|WD=Q1044103}} (about 3 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === {{Commons|Category:Kuwada-jinja (Kameoka)}} * On-site explanatory board (Yamamoto shrine only) * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Kyoto-fu no Chimei'' (Heibonsha), Kuwada Shrine entry * Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (ed.), ''Shikinaisha Chosa Hokoku'' Vol. 18, Kuwada Shrine entry {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くわたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kuwada Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kuwada Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:丹波国の式内社]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:京都府の旧無格社]] [[Category:丹波湖伝説]] [[Category:亀岡市の建築物]][[Category:qqq]]

link|Q11537594}} == Japanese content == {{Coordinates list}} '''Kuwada Shrine''' (くわだじんじゃ / くわたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} listed in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}'' for Kuwata District, {{ill|lt=Tanba Province|Q954511|WD=Q954511}}. The following two shrines are treated as candidate shrines ({{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}}): * 1. {{ill|lt=Kuwada Shrine#Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto, Kameoka)|Q11537594|WD=Q11537594|lt=Kuwada Shrine}} (Shinocho Yamamoto, {{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}) - former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} * 2. {{ill|lt=Kuwada Shrine#Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka)|Q11537594|WD=Q11537594|lt=Kuwada Shrine}} (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka, Kyoto) - former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=mukakusha|1=UNKNOWN}} === Overview === The ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' records "Kuwada Shrine, Kuwata District, Tanba Province." Two candidate shrines survive in Kameoka City. The Umahori shrine is sometimes said to be the former original site of the Yamamoto shrine, but the exact history is unclear. Both are in eastern Kameoka near the entrance to {{ill|lt=Hozukyo Gorge|Q10887680|WD=Q10887680}} on the right bank of the Oi River; {{ill|lt=Uketa Shrine|Q11631810|WD=Q11631810}} stands across the river. === Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto, Kameoka) === {{Shrine | name = Kuwada Shrine | image = [[File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) keidai.JPG|280px]]
Haiden (left) and honden (rear right) | location = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996}} Shinocho Yamamoto Kitajo 50.51 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|00|51.83|N|135|36|33.88|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|name=Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto, Kameoka)}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small), {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}}
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} | founded = Unknown | architecture = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | annual_festival = {{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|WD=Q2954}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20160304122457/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/tanba/bun/tab380102-01.html Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto Kitajo, Kameoka)] (private research site). | map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Kuwada Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|35|00|51.83|N|135|36|33.88|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|00|51.83|N|135|36|33.88|E}}}} }} '''Kuwada Shrine''' in Yamamoto is a Shinto shrine in Kameoka, Kyoto. It is a candidate Engishiki shrine; its former rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} ; Additional deities * {{ill|lt=Oyamakui|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|lt=Oyamakui-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Oyamazumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Oyamazumi-no-Mikoto}} === History === Tradition says the deities, together with those of {{ill|lt=Kuwoyama Shrine|Q11650775|WD=Q11650775}}, opened a channel through Hozukyo to reclaim the former lake area of the {{ill|lt=Kameoka Basin|Q11181997|WD=Q11181997}}. The site at the basin entrance was politically important, and the Hata clan is said to have advanced here early. Shrine tradition also links the old place-name "Tanba" to "Taniba."''Kameoka Shrine Gazetteer'', Kuwada Shrine section. The shrine buildings burned in the Tenmei era (1781-1789). Until 1873, it reportedly shared the name "Uketa Shrine (Ukita Shrine)" with the shrine across the river.On-site explanatory board. It was ranked village shrine in June 1873 and officially used the name "Kuwada Shrine" in June 1877. === Precinct === File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) honden.JPG|Honden File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) sandō.JPG|Approach File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) torii.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === * Jinushi Shrine * Gosha Shrine * Tenmangu * Suijingu * Kuzuryu Okami * Akiba-gu * Haraedo-gu === Access === * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125}} {{ill|lt=Sagano Line|Q1073011|WD=Q1073011}} {{ill|lt=Umahori Station|Q1044103|WD=Q1044103}} (about 13 minutes on foot) === Nearby === * {{ill|lt=Uketa Shrine|Q11631810|WD=Q11631810}} === Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka) === {{Shrine | name = Kuwada Shrine | image = [[File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō umahori, Kameoka) keidai.JPG|250px]]
Honden in center, Tenmangu on right | location = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996}} Shinocho Umahori Higashigaito 3 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|00|31.38|N|135|36|08.36|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|name=Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka)}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small), {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=mukakusha|1=UNKNOWN}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20160304122202/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/tanba/bun/tab380102-02.html Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori Higashigaito, Kameoka)] (private research site). | founded = Unknown | annual_festival = {{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|WD=Q2954}} | map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Kuwada Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|35|00|31.38|N|135|36|08.36|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|00|31.38|N|135|36|08.36|E}}}} }} '''Kuwada Shrine''' in Umahori is the second candidate shrine. Its former rank was mukakusha. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} === History === Its history is unclear. A tradition says the deity was moved from this site to the Yamamoto site. === Precinct === File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō umahori, Kameoka) honden.JPG|Protective housing over honden File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō umahori, Kameoka) torii.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrine === * Tenmangu === Access === * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125}} {{ill|lt=Sagano Line|Q1073011|WD=Q1073011}} {{ill|lt=Umahori Station|Q1044103|WD=Q1044103}} (about 3 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === {{Commons|Category:Kuwada-jinja (Kameoka)}} * On-site explanatory board (Yamamoto shrine only) * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Kyoto-fu no Chimei'' (Heibonsha), Kuwada Shrine entry * Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (ed.), ''Shikinaisha Chosa Hokoku'' Vol. 18, Kuwada Shrine entry {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くわたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kuwada Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kuwada Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:丹波国の式内社]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:京都府の旧無格社]] [[Category:丹波湖伝説]] [[Category:亀岡市の建築物]][[Category:qqq]]

(Kameoka)}} [[JA:桑田神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11537594}} == Japanese content == {{Coordinates list}} '''Kuwada Shrine''' (くわだじんじゃ / くわたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} listed in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' for Kuwata District, {{ill|lt=Tanba Province|Q954511|WD=Q954511|1=Tanba Province}}. The following two shrines are treated as candidate shrines ({{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}): * 1. {{ill|lt=Kuwada Shrine#Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto, Kameoka)|Q11537594|WD=Q11537594|lt=Kuwada Shrine|1=Kuwada Shrine}} (Shinocho Yamamoto, {{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}) - former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} * 2. {{ill|lt=Kuwada Shrine#Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka)|Q11537594|WD=Q11537594|lt=Kuwada Shrine|1=Kuwada Shrine}} (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka, Kyoto) - former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=mukakusha|1=UNKNOWN}} === Overview === The ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' records "Kuwada Shrine, Kuwata District, Tanba Province." Two candidate shrines survive in Kameoka City. The Umahori shrine is sometimes said to be the former original site of the Yamamoto shrine, but the exact history is unclear. Both are in eastern Kameoka near the entrance to {{ill|lt=Hozukyo Gorge|Q10887680|WD=Q10887680|1=Hozu Gorge}} on the right bank of the Oi River; {{ill|lt=Uketa Shrine|Q11631810|WD=Q11631810|1=Uketa Shrine}} stands across the river. === Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto, Kameoka) === {{Shrine | name = Kuwada Shrine | image = [[File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) keidai.JPG|280px]]
Haiden (left) and honden (rear right) | location = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}} Shinocho Yamamoto Kitajo 50.51 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|00|51.83|N|135|36|33.88|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|name=Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto, Kameoka)}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small), {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}

The site at the basin entrance was politically important, and the Hata clan is said to have advanced here early. Shrine tradition also links the old place-name "Tanba" to "Taniba."''Kameoka Shrine Gazetteer'', Kuwada Shrine section. The shrine buildings burned in the Tenmei era (1781-1789). Until 1873, it reportedly shared the name "Uketa Shrine (Ukita Shrine)" with the shrine across the river.On-site explanatory board. (Kameoka)}} [[JA:桑田神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11537594}} == Japanese content == {{Coordinates list}} '''Kuwada Shrine''' (くわだじんじゃ / くわたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} listed in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' for Kuwata District, {{ill|lt=Tanba Province|Q954511|WD=Q954511|1=Tanba Province}}. The following two shrines are treated as candidate shrines ({{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}): * 1. {{ill|lt=Kuwada Shrine#Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto, Kameoka)|Q11537594|WD=Q11537594|lt=Kuwada Shrine|1=Kuwada Shrine}} (Shinocho Yamamoto, {{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}) - former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} * 2. {{ill|lt=Kuwada Shrine#Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka)|Q11537594|WD=Q11537594|lt=Kuwada Shrine|1=Kuwada Shrine}} (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka, Kyoto) - former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=mukakusha|1=UNKNOWN}} === Overview === The ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' records "Kuwada Shrine, Kuwata District, Tanba Province." Two candidate shrines survive in Kameoka City. The Umahori shrine is sometimes said to be the former original site of the Yamamoto shrine, but the exact history is unclear. Both are in eastern Kameoka near the entrance to {{ill|lt=Hozukyo Gorge|Q10887680|WD=Q10887680|1=Hozu Gorge}} on the right bank of the Oi River; {{ill|lt=Uketa Shrine|Q11631810|WD=Q11631810|1=Uketa Shrine}} stands across the river. === Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto, Kameoka) === {{Shrine | name = Kuwada Shrine | image = [[File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) keidai.JPG|280px]]
Haiden (left) and honden (rear right) | location = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}} Shinocho Yamamoto Kitajo 50.51 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|00|51.83|N|135|36|33.88|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|name=Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto, Kameoka)}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small), {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | founded = Unknown | architecture = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | annual_festival = {{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|WD=Q2954|1=October 25}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20160304122457/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/tanba/bun/tab380102-01.html Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto Kitajo, Kameoka)] (private research site). | map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Kuwada Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|35|00|51.83|N|135|36|33.88|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|00|51.83|N|135|36|33.88|E}}}} }} '''Kuwada Shrine''' in Yamamoto is a Shinto shrine in Kameoka, Kyoto. It is a candidate Engishiki shrine; its former rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} ; Additional deities * {{ill|lt=Oyamakui|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|lt=Oyamakui-no-Mikoto|1=Oyamakui no Kami}} * {{ill|lt=Oyamazumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Oyamazumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} === History === Tradition says the deities, together with those of {{ill|lt=Kuwoyama Shrine|Q11650775|WD=Q11650775|1=Kuwayama Shrine}}, opened a channel through Hozukyo to reclaim the former lake area of the {{ill|lt=Kameoka Basin|Q11181997|WD=Q11181997|1=Kameoka Basin}}. The site at the basin entrance was politically important, and the Hata clan is said to have advanced here early. Shrine tradition also links the old place-name "Tanba" to "Taniba."''Kameoka Shrine Gazetteer'', Kuwada Shrine section. The shrine buildings burned in the Tenmei era (1781-1789). Until 1873, it reportedly shared the name "Uketa Shrine (Ukita Shrine)" with the shrine across the river.On-site explanatory board. It was ranked village shrine in June 1873 and officially used the name "Kuwada Shrine" in June 1877. === Precinct === File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) honden.JPG|Honden File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) sandō.JPG|Approach File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) torii.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === * Jinushi Shrine * Gosha Shrine * Tenmangu * Suijingu * Kuzuryu Okami * Akiba-gu * Haraedo-gu === Access === * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Sagano Line|Q1073011|WD=Q1073011|1=Sagano Line}} {{ill|lt=Umahori Station|Q1044103|WD=Q1044103|1=Umahori Station}} (about 13 minutes on foot) === Nearby === * {{ill|lt=Uketa Shrine|Q11631810|WD=Q11631810|1=Uketa Shrine}} === Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka) === {{Shrine | name = Kuwada Shrine | image = [[File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō umahori, Kameoka) keidai.JPG|250px]]
Honden in center, Tenmangu on right | location = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}} Shinocho Umahori Higashigaito 3 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|00|31.38|N|135|36|08.36|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|name=Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka)}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small), {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=mukakusha|1=UNKNOWN}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20160304122202/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/tanba/bun/tab380102-02.html Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori Higashigaito, Kameoka)] (private research site). | founded = Unknown | annual_festival = {{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|WD=Q2954|1=October 25}} | map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Kuwada Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|35|00|31.38|N|135|36|08.36|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|00|31.38|N|135|36|08.36|E}}}} }} '''Kuwada Shrine''' in Umahori is the second candidate shrine. Its former rank was mukakusha. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} === History === Its history is unclear. A tradition says the deity was moved from this site to the Yamamoto site. === Precinct === File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō umahori, Kameoka) honden.JPG|Protective housing over honden File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō umahori, Kameoka) torii.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrine === * Tenmangu === Access === * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Sagano Line|Q1073011|WD=Q1073011|1=Sagano Line}} {{ill|lt=Umahori Station|Q1044103|WD=Q1044103|1=Umahori Station}} (about 3 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === {{Commons|Category:Kuwada-jinja (Kameoka)}} * On-site explanatory board (Yamamoto shrine only) * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Kyoto-fu no Chimei'' (Heibonsha), Kuwada Shrine entry * Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (ed.), ''Shikinaisha Chosa Hokoku'' Vol. 18, Kuwada Shrine entry {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くわたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kuwada Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kuwada Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:丹波国の式内社]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:京都府の旧無格社]] [[Category:丹波湖伝説]] [[Category:亀岡市の建築物]][[Category:qqq]]

The following two shrines are treated as candidate shrines ({{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}): * 1. {{ill|lt=Kuwada Shrine#Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto, Kameoka)|Q11537594|WD=Q11537594|lt=Kuwada Shrine|1=Kuwada Shrine}} (Shinocho Yamamoto, {{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}) - former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} * 2. {{ill|lt=Kuwada Shrine#Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka)|Q11537594|WD=Q11537594|lt=Kuwada Shrine|1=Kuwada Shrine}} (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka, Kyoto) - former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=mukakusha|1=UNKNOWN}} === Overview === The ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' records "Kuwada Shrine, Kuwata District, Tanba Province." Two candidate shrines survive in Kameoka City. The Umahori shrine is sometimes said to be the former original site of the Yamamoto shrine, but the exact history is unclear. Both are in eastern Kameoka near the entrance to {{ill|lt=Hozukyo Gorge|Q10887680|WD=Q10887680|1=Hozu Gorge}} on the right bank of the Oi River; {{ill|lt=Uketa Shrine|Q11631810|WD=Q11631810|1=Uketa Shrine}} stands across the river. === Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto, Kameoka) === {{Shrine | name = Kuwada Shrine | image = [[File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) keidai.JPG|280px]]
Haiden (left) and honden (rear right) | location = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}} Shinocho Yamamoto Kitajo 50.51 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|00|51.83|N|135|36|33.88|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|name=Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto, Kameoka)}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small), {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | founded = Unknown | architecture = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | annual_festival = {{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|WD=Q2954|1=October 25}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20160304122457/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/tanba/bun/tab380102-01.html Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto Kitajo, Kameoka)] (private research site). | map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Kuwada Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|35|00|51.83|N|135|36|33.88|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|00|51.83|N|135|36|33.88|E}}}} }} '''Kuwada Shrine''' in Yamamoto is a Shinto shrine in Kameoka, Kyoto. It is a candidate Engishiki shrine; its former rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} ; Additional deities * {{ill|lt=Oyamakui|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|lt=Oyamakui-no-Mikoto|1=Oyamakui no Kami}} * {{ill|lt=Oyamazumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Oyamazumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} === History === Tradition says the deities, together with those of {{ill|lt=Kuwoyama Shrine|Q11650775|WD=Q11650775|1=Kuwayama Shrine}}, opened a channel through Hozukyo to reclaim the former lake area of the {{ill|lt=Kameoka Basin|Q11181997|WD=Q11181997|1=Kameoka Basin}}. The site at the basin entrance was politically important, and the Hata clan is said to have advanced here early. Shrine tradition also links the old place-name "Tanba" to "Taniba."''Kameoka Shrine Gazetteer'', Kuwada Shrine section. The shrine buildings burned in the Tenmei era (1781-1789). Until 1873, it reportedly shared the name "Uketa Shrine (Ukita Shrine)" with the shrine across the river.On-site explanatory board. It was ranked village shrine in June 1873 and officially used the name "Kuwada Shrine" in June 1877. === Precinct === File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) honden.JPG|Honden File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) sandō.JPG|Approach File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) torii.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === * Jinushi Shrine * Gosha Shrine * Tenmangu * Suijingu * Kuzuryu Okami * Akiba-gu * Haraedo-gu === Access === * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Sagano Line|Q1073011|WD=Q1073011|1=Sagano Line}} {{ill|lt=Umahori Station|Q1044103|WD=Q1044103|1=Umahori Station}} (about 13 minutes on foot) === Nearby === * {{ill|lt=Uketa Shrine|Q11631810|WD=Q11631810|1=Uketa Shrine}} === Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka) === {{Shrine | name = Kuwada Shrine | image = [[File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō umahori, Kameoka) keidai.JPG|250px]]
Honden in center, Tenmangu on right | location = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}} Shinocho Umahori Higashigaito 3 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|00|31.38|N|135|36|08.36|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|name=Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka)}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small), {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=mukakusha|1=UNKNOWN}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20160304122202/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/tanba/bun/tab380102-02.html Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori Higashigaito, Kameoka)] (private research site). | founded = Unknown | annual_festival = {{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|WD=Q2954|1=October 25}} | map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Kuwada Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|35|00|31.38|N|135|36|08.36|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|00|31.38|N|135|36|08.36|E}}}} }} '''Kuwada Shrine''' in Umahori is the second candidate shrine. Its former rank was mukakusha. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} === History === Its history is unclear. A tradition says the deity was moved from this site to the Yamamoto site. === Precinct === File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō umahori, Kameoka) honden.JPG|Protective housing over honden File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō umahori, Kameoka) torii.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrine === * Tenmangu === Access === * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Sagano Line|Q1073011|WD=Q1073011|1=Sagano Line}} {{ill|lt=Umahori Station|Q1044103|WD=Q1044103|1=Umahori Station}} (about 3 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === {{Commons|Category:Kuwada-jinja (Kameoka)}} * On-site explanatory board (Yamamoto shrine only) * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Kyoto-fu no Chimei'' (Heibonsha), Kuwada Shrine entry * Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (ed.), ''Shikinaisha Chosa Hokoku'' Vol. 18, Kuwada Shrine entry {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くわたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kuwada Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kuwada Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:丹波国の式内社]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:京都府の旧無格社]] [[Category:丹波湖伝説]] [[Category:亀岡市の建築物]]

(Kameoka)}} [[JA:桑田神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11537594}} == Japanese content == {{Coordinates list}} '''Kuwada Shrine''' (くわだじんじゃ / くわたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} listed in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' for Kuwata District, {{ill|lt=Tanba Province|Q954511|WD=Q954511|1=Tanba Province}}. The following two shrines are treated as candidate shrines ({{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}): * 1. {{ill|lt=Kuwada Shrine#Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto, Kameoka)|Q11537594|WD=Q11537594|lt=Kuwada Shrine|1=Kuwada Shrine}} (Shinocho Yamamoto, {{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}) - former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} * 2. {{ill|lt=Kuwada Shrine#Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka)|Q11537594|WD=Q11537594|lt=Kuwada Shrine|1=Kuwada Shrine}} (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka, Kyoto) - former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=mukakusha|1=UNKNOWN}} === Overview === The ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' records "Kuwada Shrine, Kuwata District, Tanba Province." Two candidate shrines survive in Kameoka City. The Umahori shrine is sometimes said to be the former original site of the Yamamoto shrine, but the exact history is unclear. Both are in eastern Kameoka near the entrance to {{ill|lt=Hozukyo Gorge|Q10887680|WD=Q10887680|1=Hozu Gorge}} on the right bank of the Oi River; {{ill|lt=Uketa Shrine|Q11631810|WD=Q11631810|1=Uketa Shrine}} stands across the river. === Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto, Kameoka) === {{Shrine | name = Kuwada Shrine | image = [[File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) keidai.JPG|280px]]
Haiden (left) and honden (rear right) | location = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}} Shinocho Yamamoto Kitajo 50.51 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|00|51.83|N|135|36|33.88|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|name=Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto, Kameoka)}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small), {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | founded = Unknown | architecture = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | annual_festival = {{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|WD=Q2954|1=October 25}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20160304122457/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/tanba/bun/tab380102-01.html Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto Kitajo, Kameoka)] (private research site). | map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Kuwada Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|35|00|51.83|N|135|36|33.88|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|00|51.83|N|135|36|33.88|E}}}} }} '''Kuwada Shrine''' in Yamamoto is a Shinto shrine in Kameoka, Kyoto. It is a candidate Engishiki shrine; its former rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} ; Additional deities * {{ill|lt=Oyamakui|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|lt=Oyamakui-no-Mikoto|1=Oyamakui no Kami}} * {{ill|lt=Oyamazumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Oyamazumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} === History === Tradition says the deities, together with those of {{ill|lt=Kuwoyama Shrine|Q11650775|WD=Q11650775|1=Kuwayama Shrine}}, opened a channel through Hozukyo to reclaim the former lake area of the {{ill|lt=Kameoka Basin|Q11181997|WD=Q11181997|1=Kameoka Basin}}. The site at the basin entrance was politically important, and the Hata clan is said to have advanced here early. Shrine tradition also links the old place-name "Tanba" to "Taniba."''Kameoka Shrine Gazetteer'', Kuwada Shrine section. The shrine buildings burned in the Tenmei era (1781-1789). Until 1873, it reportedly shared the name "Uketa Shrine (Ukita Shrine)" with the shrine across the river.On-site explanatory board. It was ranked village shrine in June 1873 and officially used the name "Kuwada Shrine" in June 1877. === Precinct === File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) honden.JPG|Honden File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) sandō.JPG|Approach File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) torii.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === * Jinushi Shrine * Gosha Shrine * Tenmangu * Suijingu * Kuzuryu Okami * Akiba-gu * Haraedo-gu === Access === * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Sagano Line|Q1073011|WD=Q1073011|1=Sagano Line}} {{ill|lt=Umahori Station|Q1044103|WD=Q1044103|1=Umahori Station}} (about 13 minutes on foot) === Nearby === * {{ill|lt=Uketa Shrine|Q11631810|WD=Q11631810|1=Uketa Shrine}} === Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka) === {{Shrine | name = Kuwada Shrine | image = [[File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō umahori, Kameoka) keidai.JPG|250px]]
Honden in center, Tenmangu on right | location = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}} Shinocho Umahori Higashigaito 3 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|00|31.38|N|135|36|08.36|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|name=Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka)}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small), {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=mukakusha|1=UNKNOWN}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20160304122202/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/tanba/bun/tab380102-02.html Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori Higashigaito, Kameoka)] (private research site). | founded = Unknown | annual_festival = {{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|WD=Q2954|1=October 25}} | map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Kuwada Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|35|00|31.38|N|135|36|08.36|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|00|31.38|N|135|36|08.36|E}}}} }} '''Kuwada Shrine''' in Umahori is the second candidate shrine. Its former rank was mukakusha. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} === History === Its history is unclear. A tradition says the deity was moved from this site to the Yamamoto site. === Precinct === File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō umahori, Kameoka) honden.JPG|Protective housing over honden File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō umahori, Kameoka) torii.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrine === * Tenmangu === Access === * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Sagano Line|Q1073011|WD=Q1073011|1=Sagano Line}} {{ill|lt=Umahori Station|Q1044103|WD=Q1044103|1=Umahori Station}} (about 3 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === {{Commons|Category:Kuwada-jinja (Kameoka)}} * On-site explanatory board (Yamamoto shrine only) * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Kyoto-fu no Chimei'' (Heibonsha), Kuwada Shrine entry * Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (ed.), ''Shikinaisha Chosa Hokoku'' Vol. 18, Kuwada Shrine entry {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kuwada Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kuwada Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:丹波国の式内社]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:京都府の旧無格社]] [[Category:丹波湖伝説]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kameoka, Kyoto]]

(Kameoka)}} [[JA:桑田神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11537594}} == Japanese content == {{Coordinates list}} '''Kuwada Shrine''' (くわだじんじゃ / くわたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} listed in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' for Kuwata District, {{ill|lt=Tanba Province|Q954511|WD=Q954511|1=Tanba Province}}. The following two shrines are treated as candidate shrines ({{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}): * 1. {{ill|lt=Kuwada Shrine#Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto, Kameoka)|Q11537594|WD=Q11537594|lt=Kuwada Shrine|1=Kuwada Shrine}} (Shinocho Yamamoto, {{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}) - former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} * 2. {{ill|lt=Kuwada Shrine#Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka)|Q11537594|WD=Q11537594|lt=Kuwada Shrine|1=Kuwada Shrine}} (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka, Kyoto) - former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=mukakusha|1=UNKNOWN}} === Overview === The ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' records "Kuwada Shrine, Kuwata District, Tanba Province." Two candidate shrines survive in Kameoka City. The Umahori shrine is sometimes said to be the former original site of the Yamamoto shrine, but the exact history is unclear. Both are in eastern Kameoka near the entrance to {{ill|lt=Hozukyo Gorge|Q10887680|WD=Q10887680|1=Hozu Gorge}} on the right bank of the Oi River; {{ill|lt=Uketa Shrine|Q11631810|WD=Q11631810|1=Uketa Shrine}} stands across the river. === Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto, Kameoka) === {{Shrine | name = Kuwada Shrine | image = [[File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) keidai.JPG|280px]]
Haiden (left) and honden (rear right) | location = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}} Shinocho Yamamoto Kitajo 50.51 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|00|51.83|N|135|36|33.88|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|name=Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto, Kameoka)}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small), {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | founded = Unknown | architecture = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | annual_festival = {{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|WD=Q2954|1=October 25}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20160304122457/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/tanba/bun/tab380102-01.html Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto Kitajo, Kameoka)] (private research site). | map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Kuwada Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|35|00|51.83|N|135|36|33.88|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|00|51.83|N|135|36|33.88|E}}}} }} '''Kuwada Shrine''' in Yamamoto is a Shinto shrine in Kameoka, Kyoto. It is a candidate Engishiki shrine; its former rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} ; Additional deities * {{ill|lt=Oyamakui|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|lt=Oyamakui-no-Mikoto|1=Oyamakui no Kami}} * {{ill|lt=Oyamazumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Oyamazumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} === History === Tradition says the deities, together with those of {{ill|lt=Kuwoyama Shrine|Q11650775|WD=Q11650775|1=Kuwayama Shrine}}, opened a channel through Hozukyo to reclaim the former lake area of the {{ill|lt=Kameoka Basin|Q11181997|WD=Q11181997|1=Kameoka Basin}}. The site at the basin entrance was politically important, and the Hata clan is said to have advanced here early. Shrine tradition also links the old place-name "Tanba" to "Taniba."''Kameoka Shrine Gazetteer'', Kuwada Shrine section. The shrine buildings burned in the Tenmei era (1781-1789). Until 1873, it reportedly shared the name "Uketa Shrine (Ukita Shrine)" with the shrine across the river.On-site explanatory board. It was ranked village shrine in June 1873 and officially used the name "Kuwada Shrine" in June 1877. === Precinct === File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) honden.JPG|Honden File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) sandō.JPG|Approach File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) torii.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === * Jinushi Shrine * Gosha Shrine * Tenmangu * Suijingu * Kuzuryu Okami * Akiba-gu * Haraedo-gu === Access === * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Sagano Line|Q1073011|WD=Q1073011|1=Sagano Line}} {{ill|lt=Umahori Station|Q1044103|WD=Q1044103|1=Umahori Station}} (about 13 minutes on foot) === Nearby === * {{ill|lt=Uketa Shrine|Q11631810|WD=Q11631810|1=Uketa Shrine}} === Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka) === {{Shrine | name = Kuwada Shrine | image = [[File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō umahori, Kameoka) keidai.JPG|250px]]
Honden in center, Tenmangu on right | location = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}} Shinocho Umahori Higashigaito 3 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|00|31.38|N|135|36|08.36|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|name=Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka)}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small), {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=mukakusha|1=UNKNOWN}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20160304122202/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/tanba/bun/tab380102-02.html Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori Higashigaito, Kameoka)] (private research site). | founded = Unknown | annual_festival = {{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|WD=Q2954|1=October 25}} | map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Kuwada Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|35|00|31.38|N|135|36|08.36|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|00|31.38|N|135|36|08.36|E}}}} }} '''Kuwada Shrine''' in Umahori is the second candidate shrine. Its former rank was mukakusha. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} === History === Its history is unclear. A tradition says the deity was moved from this site to the Yamamoto site. === Precinct === File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō umahori, Kameoka) honden.JPG|Protective housing over honden File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō umahori, Kameoka) torii.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrine === * Tenmangu === Access === * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Sagano Line|Q1073011|WD=Q1073011|1=Sagano Line}} {{ill|lt=Umahori Station|Q1044103|WD=Q1044103|1=Umahori Station}} (about 3 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === {{Commons|Category:Kuwada-jinja (Kameoka)}} * On-site explanatory board (Yamamoto shrine only) * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Kyoto-fu no Chimei'' (Heibonsha), Kuwada Shrine entry * Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (ed.), ''Shikinaisha Chosa Hokoku'' Vol. 18, Kuwada Shrine entry {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kuwada Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kuwada Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:丹波国の式内社]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:京都府の旧無格社]] [[Category:丹波湖伝説]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kameoka, Kyoto]]

Both are in eastern Kameoka near the entrance to {{ill|lt=Hozukyo Gorge|Q10887680|WD=Q10887680|1=Hozu Gorge}} on the right bank of the Oi River; {{ill|lt=Uketa Shrine|Q11631810|WD=Q11631810|1=Uketa Shrine}} stands across the river. === Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto, Kameoka) === {{Shrine | name = Kuwada Shrine | image = [[File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) keidai.JPG|280px]]

former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | founded = Unknown | architecture = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | annual_festival = {{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|WD=Q2954|1=October 25}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20160304122457/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/tanba/bun/tab380102-01.html Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto Kitajo, Kameoka)] (private research site). | map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Kuwada Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|35|00|51.83|N|135|36|33.88|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|00|51.83|N|135|36|33.88|E}}}} }} '''Kuwada Shrine''' in Yamamoto is a Shinto shrine in Kameoka, Kyoto. It is a candidate Engishiki shrine; its former rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} ; Additional deities * {{ill|lt=Oyamakui|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|lt=Oyamakui-no-Mikoto|1=Oyamakui no Kami}} * {{ill|lt=Oyamazumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Oyamazumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} === History === Tradition says the deities, together with those of {{ill|lt=Kuwoyama Shrine|Q11650775|WD=Q11650775|1=Kuwayama Shrine}}, opened a channel through Hozukyo to reclaim the former lake area of the {{ill|lt=Kameoka Basin|Q11181997|WD=Q11181997|1=Kameoka Basin}}. The site at the basin entrance was politically important, and the Hata clan is said to have advanced here early. Shrine tradition also links the old place-name "Tanba" to "Taniba."''Kameoka Shrine Gazetteer'', Kuwada Shrine section. The shrine buildings burned in the Tenmei era (1781-1789). Until 1873, it reportedly shared the name "Uketa Shrine (Ukita Shrine)" with the shrine across the river.On-site explanatory board. It was ranked village shrine in June 1873 and officially used the name "Kuwada Shrine" in June 1877. === Precinct === File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) honden.JPG|Honden File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) sandō.JPG|Approach File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) torii.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === * Jinushi Shrine * Gosha Shrine * Tenmangu * Suijingu * Kuzuryu Okami * Akiba-gu * Haraedo-gu === Access === * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Sagano Line|Q1073011|WD=Q1073011|1=Sagano Line}} {{ill|lt=Umahori Station|Q1044103|WD=Q1044103|1=Umahori Station}} (about 13 minutes on foot) === Nearby === * {{ill|lt=Uketa Shrine|Q11631810|WD=Q11631810|1=Uketa Shrine}} === Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka) === {{Shrine | name = Kuwada Shrine | image = [[File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō umahori, Kameoka) keidai.JPG|250px]]
Honden in center, Tenmangu on right | location = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}} Shinocho Umahori Higashigaito 3 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|00|31.38|N|135|36|08.36|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|name=Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka)}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small), {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=mukakusha|1=UNKNOWN}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20160304122202/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/tanba/bun/tab380102-02.html Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori Higashigaito, Kameoka)] (private research site). | founded = Unknown | annual_festival = {{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|WD=Q2954|1=October 25}} | map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Kuwada Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|35|00|31.38|N|135|36|08.36|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|00|31.38|N|135|36|08.36|E}}}} }} '''Kuwada Shrine''' in Umahori is the second candidate shrine. Its former rank was mukakusha. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} === History === Its history is unclear. A tradition says the deity was moved from this site to the Yamamoto site. === Precinct === File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō umahori, Kameoka) honden.JPG|Protective housing over honden File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō umahori, Kameoka) torii.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrine === * Tenmangu === Access === * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Sagano Line|Q1073011|WD=Q1073011|1=Sagano Line}} {{ill|lt=Umahori Station|Q1044103|WD=Q1044103|1=Umahori Station}} (about 3 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === {{Commons|Category:Kuwada-jinja (Kameoka)}} * On-site explanatory board (Yamamoto shrine only) * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Kyoto-fu no Chimei'' (Heibonsha), Kuwada Shrine entry * Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (ed.), ''Shikinaisha Chosa Hokoku'' Vol. 18, Kuwada Shrine entry {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kuwada Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kuwada Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:京都府の旧無格社]] [[Category:丹波湖伝説]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kameoka, Kyoto]]

(Kameoka)}} [[JA:桑田神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11537594}} == Japanese content == {{Coordinates list}} '''Kuwada Shrine''' (くわだじんじゃ / くわたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} listed in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' for Kuwata District, {{ill|lt=Tanba Province|Q954511|WD=Q954511|1=Tanba Province}}. The following two shrines are treated as candidate shrines ({{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}): * 1. {{ill|lt=Kuwada Shrine#Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto, Kameoka)|Q11537594|WD=Q11537594|lt=Kuwada Shrine|1=Kuwada Shrine}} (Shinocho Yamamoto, {{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}) - former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} * 2. {{ill|lt=Kuwada Shrine#Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka)|Q11537594|WD=Q11537594|lt=Kuwada Shrine|1=Kuwada Shrine}} (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka, Kyoto) - former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=mukakusha|1=UNKNOWN}} === Overview === The ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' records "Kuwada Shrine, Kuwata District, Tanba Province." Two candidate shrines survive in Kameoka City. The Umahori shrine is sometimes said to be the former original site of the Yamamoto shrine, but the exact history is unclear. Both are in eastern Kameoka near the entrance to {{ill|lt=Hozukyo Gorge|Q10887680|WD=Q10887680|1=Hozu Gorge}} on the right bank of the Oi River; {{ill|lt=Uketa Shrine|Q11631810|WD=Q11631810|1=Uketa Shrine}} stands across the river. === Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto, Kameoka) === {{Shrine | name = Kuwada Shrine | image = [[File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) keidai.JPG|280px]]

The site at the basin entrance was politically important, and the Hata clan is said to have advanced here early. Shrine tradition also links the old place-name "Tanba" to "Taniba."''Kameoka Shrine Gazetteer'', Kuwada Shrine section. The shrine buildings burned in the Tenmei era (1781-1789). (Kameoka)}} [[JA:桑田神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11537594}} == Japanese content == {{Coordinates list}} '''Kuwada Shrine''' (くわだじんじゃ / くわたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} listed in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' for Kuwata District, {{ill|lt=Tanba Province|Q954511|WD=Q954511|1=Tanba Province}}. The following two shrines are treated as candidate shrines ({{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}): * 1. {{ill|lt=Kuwada Shrine#Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto, Kameoka)|Q11537594|WD=Q11537594|lt=Kuwada Shrine|1=Kuwada Shrine}} (Shinocho Yamamoto, {{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}) - former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} * 2. {{ill|lt=Kuwada Shrine#Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka)|Q11537594|WD=Q11537594|lt=Kuwada Shrine|1=Kuwada Shrine}} (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka, Kyoto) - former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=mukakusha|1=UNKNOWN}} === Overview === The ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' records "Kuwada Shrine, Kuwata District, Tanba Province." Two candidate shrines survive in Kameoka City. The Umahori shrine is sometimes said to be the former original site of the Yamamoto shrine, but the exact history is unclear. Both are in eastern Kameoka near the entrance to {{ill|lt=Hozukyo Gorge|Q10887680|WD=Q10887680|1=Hozu Gorge}} on the right bank of the Oi River; {{ill|lt=Uketa Shrine|Q11631810|WD=Q11631810|1=Uketa Shrine}} stands across the river. === Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto, Kameoka) === {{Shrine | name = Kuwada Shrine | image = [[File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) keidai.JPG|280px]]
Haiden (left) and honden (rear right) | location = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}} Shinocho Yamamoto Kitajo 50.51 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|00|51.83|N|135|36|33.88|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|name=Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto, Kameoka)}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small), {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | founded = Unknown | architecture = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | annual_festival = {{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|WD=Q2954|1=October 25}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20160304122457/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/tanba/bun/tab380102-01.html Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto Kitajo, Kameoka)] (private research site). | map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Kuwada Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|35|00|51.83|N|135|36|33.88|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|00|51.83|N|135|36|33.88|E}}}} }} '''Kuwada Shrine''' in Yamamoto is a Shinto shrine in Kameoka, Kyoto. It is a candidate Engishiki shrine; its former rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} ; Additional deities * {{ill|lt=Oyamakui|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|lt=Oyamakui-no-Mikoto|1=Oyamakui no Kami}} * {{ill|lt=Oyamazumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Oyamazumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} === History === Tradition says the deities, together with those of {{ill|lt=Kuwoyama Shrine|Q11650775|WD=Q11650775|1=Kuwayama Shrine}}, opened a channel through Hozukyo to reclaim the former lake area of the {{ill|lt=Kameoka Basin|Q11181997|WD=Q11181997|1=Kameoka Basin}}. The site at the basin entrance was politically important, and the Hata clan is said to have advanced here early. Shrine tradition also links the old place-name "Tanba" to "Taniba."''Kameoka Shrine Gazetteer'', Kuwada Shrine section. The shrine buildings burned in the Tenmei era (1781-1789). Until 1873, it reportedly shared the name "Uketa Shrine (Ukita Shrine)" with the shrine across the river.On-site explanatory board. It was ranked village shrine in June 1873 and officially used the name "Kuwada Shrine" in June 1877. === Precinct === File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) honden.JPG|Honden File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) sandō.JPG|Approach File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) torii.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === * Jinushi Shrine * Gosha Shrine * Tenmangu * Suijingu * Kuzuryu Okami * Akiba-gu * Haraedo-gu === Access === * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Sagano Line|Q1073011|WD=Q1073011|1=Sagano Line}} {{ill|lt=Umahori Station|Q1044103|WD=Q1044103|1=Umahori Station}} (about 13 minutes on foot) === Nearby === * {{ill|lt=Uketa Shrine|Q11631810|WD=Q11631810|1=Uketa Shrine}} === Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka) === {{Shrine | name = Kuwada Shrine | image = [[File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō umahori, Kameoka) keidai.JPG|250px]]
Honden in center, Tenmangu on right | location = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}} Shinocho Umahori Higashigaito 3 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|00|31.38|N|135|36|08.36|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|name=Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka)}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small), {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=mukakusha|1=UNKNOWN}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20160304122202/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/tanba/bun/tab380102-02.html Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori Higashigaito, Kameoka)] (private research site). | founded = Unknown | annual_festival = {{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|WD=Q2954|1=October 25}} | map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Kuwada Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|35|00|31.38|N|135|36|08.36|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|00|31.38|N|135|36|08.36|E}}}} }} '''Kuwada Shrine''' in Umahori is the second candidate shrine. Its former rank was mukakusha. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} === History === Its history is unclear. A tradition says the deity was moved from this site to the Yamamoto site. === Precinct === File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō umahori, Kameoka) honden.JPG|Protective housing over honden File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō umahori, Kameoka) torii.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrine === * Tenmangu === Access === * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Sagano Line|Q1073011|WD=Q1073011|1=Sagano Line}} {{ill|lt=Umahori Station|Q1044103|WD=Q1044103|1=Umahori Station}} (about 3 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === {{Commons|Category:Kuwada-jinja (Kameoka)}} * On-site explanatory board (Yamamoto shrine only) * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Kyoto-fu no Chimei'' (Heibonsha), Kuwada Shrine entry * Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (ed.), ''Shikinaisha Chosa Hokoku'' Vol. 18, Kuwada Shrine entry {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kuwada Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kuwada Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Unranked Shrines in Kyōto Prefecture]] [[Category:丹波湖伝説]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kameoka, Kyoto]]

(Kameoka)}} [[JA:桑田神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11537594}} == Japanese content == {{Coordinates list}} '''Kuwada Shrine''' (くわだじんじゃ / くわたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} listed in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' for Kuwata District, {{ill|lt=Tanba Province|Q954511|WD=Q954511|1=Tanba Province}}. The following two shrines are treated as candidate shrines ({{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}): * 1. {{ill|lt=Kuwada Shrine#Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto, Kameoka)|Q11537594|WD=Q11537594|lt=Kuwada Shrine|1=Kuwada Shrine}} (Shinocho Yamamoto, {{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}) - former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} * 2. {{ill|lt=Kuwada Shrine#Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka)|Q11537594|WD=Q11537594|lt=Kuwada Shrine|1=Kuwada Shrine}} (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka, Kyoto) - former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=mukakusha|1=UNKNOWN}} === Overview === The ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' records "Kuwada Shrine, Kuwata District, Tanba Province." Two candidate shrines survive in Kameoka City. The Umahori shrine is sometimes said to be the former original site of the Yamamoto shrine, but the exact history is unclear. Both are in eastern Kameoka near the entrance to {{ill|lt=Hozukyo Gorge|Q10887680|WD=Q10887680|1=Hozu Gorge}} on the right bank of the Oi River; {{ill|lt=Uketa Shrine|Q11631810|WD=Q11631810|1=Uketa Shrine}} stands across the river. === Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto, Kameoka) === {{Shrine | name = Kuwada Shrine | image = [[File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) keidai.JPG|280px]]
Haiden (left) and honden (rear right) | location = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}} Shinocho Yamamoto Kitajo 50.51 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|00|51.83|N|135|36|33.88|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|name=Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto, Kameoka)}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small), {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | founded = Unknown | architecture = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | annual_festival = {{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|WD=Q2954|1=October 25}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20160304122457/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/tanba/bun/tab380102-01.html Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Yamamoto Kitajo, Kameoka)] (private research site). | map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Kuwada Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|35|00|51.83|N|135|36|33.88|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|00|51.83|N|135|36|33.88|E}}}} }} '''Kuwada Shrine''' in Yamamoto is a Shinto shrine in Kameoka, Kyoto. It is a candidate Engishiki shrine; its former rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} ; Additional deities * {{ill|lt=Oyamakui|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|lt=Oyamakui-no-Mikoto|1=Oyamakui no Kami}} * {{ill|lt=Oyamazumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Oyamazumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} === History === Tradition says the deities, together with those of {{ill|lt=Kuwoyama Shrine|Q11650775|WD=Q11650775|1=Kuwayama Shrine}}, opened a channel through Hozukyo to reclaim the former lake area of the {{ill|lt=Kameoka Basin|Q11181997|WD=Q11181997|1=Kameoka Basin}}. The site at the basin entrance was politically important, and the Hata clan is said to have advanced here early. Shrine tradition also links the old place-name "Tanba" to "Taniba."''Kameoka Shrine Gazetteer'', Kuwada Shrine section. The shrine buildings burned in the Tenmei era (1781-1789). Until 1873, it reportedly shared the name "Uketa Shrine (Ukita Shrine)" with the shrine across the river.On-site explanatory board. It was ranked village shrine in June 1873 and officially used the name "Kuwada Shrine" in June 1877. === Precinct === File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) honden.JPG|Honden File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) sandō.JPG|Approach File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) torii.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === * Jinushi Shrine * Gosha Shrine * Tenmangu * Suijingu * Kuzuryu Okami * Akiba-gu * Haraedo-gu === Access === * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Sagano Line|Q1073011|WD=Q1073011|1=Sagano Line}} {{ill|lt=Umahori Station|Q1044103|WD=Q1044103|1=Umahori Station}} (about 13 minutes on foot) === Nearby === * {{ill|lt=Uketa Shrine|Q11631810|WD=Q11631810|1=Uketa Shrine}} === Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka) === {{Shrine | name = Kuwada Shrine | image = [[File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō umahori, Kameoka) keidai.JPG|250px]]
Honden in center, Tenmangu on right | location = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}} Shinocho Umahori Higashigaito 3 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|00|31.38|N|135|36|08.36|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|name=Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka)}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small), {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=mukakusha|1=UNKNOWN}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20160304122202/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/tanba/bun/tab380102-02.html Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori Higashigaito, Kameoka)] (private research site). | founded = Unknown | annual_festival = {{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|WD=Q2954|1=October 25}} | map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Kuwada Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|35|00|31.38|N|135|36|08.36|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|00|31.38|N|135|36|08.36|E}}}} }} '''Kuwada Shrine''' in Umahori is the second candidate shrine. Its former rank was mukakusha. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} === History === Its history is unclear. A tradition says the deity was moved from this site to the Yamamoto site. === Precinct === File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō umahori, Kameoka) honden.JPG|Protective housing over honden File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō umahori, Kameoka) torii.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrine === * Tenmangu === Access === * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Sagano Line|Q1073011|WD=Q1073011|1=Sagano Line}} {{ill|lt=Umahori Station|Q1044103|WD=Q1044103|1=Umahori Station}} (about 3 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === {{Commons|Category:Kuwada-jinja (Kameoka)}} * On-site explanatory board (Yamamoto shrine only) * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Kyoto-fu no Chimei'' (Heibonsha), Kuwada Shrine entry * Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (ed.), ''Shikinaisha Chosa Hokoku'' Vol. 18, Kuwada Shrine entry {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kuwada Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kuwada Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Unranked Shrines in Kyōto Prefecture]] [[Category:丹波湖伝説]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kameoka, Kyoto]]

It was ranked village shrine in June 1873 and officially used the name "Kuwada Shrine" in June 1877. === Precinct === File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) honden.JPG|Honden File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) sandō.JPG|Approach File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō yamamoto, Kameoka) torii.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === * Jinushi Shrine * Gosha Shrine * Tenmangu * Suijingu * Kuzuryu Okami * Akiba-gu * Haraedo-gu === Access === * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|qid=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Sagano Line|Q1073011|qid=Q1073011|1=Sagano Line}} {{ill|lt=Umahori Station|Q1044103|qid=Q1044103|1=Umahori Station}} (about 13 minutes on foot) === Nearby === * {{ill|lt=Uketa Shrine|Q11631810|qid=Q11631810|1=Uketa Shrine}} === Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka) === {{Shrine | name = Kuwada Shrine | image = [[File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō umahori, Kameoka) keidai.JPG|250px]]
Honden in center, Tenmangu on right | location = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|qid=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|qid=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}} Shinocho Umahori Higashigaito 3 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|00|31.38|N|135|36|08.36|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|name=Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori, Kameoka)}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|qid=Q11059454|qid=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (small), {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=mukakusha|1=UNKNOWN}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20160304122202/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/tanba/bun/tab380102-02.html Kuwada Shrine (Shinocho Umahori Higashigaito, Kameoka)] (private research site). | founded = Unknown | annual_festival = {{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|qid=Q2954|1=October 25}} | map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Kuwada Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|35|00|31.38|N|135|36|08.36|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|00|31.38|N|135|36|08.36|E}}}} }} '''Kuwada Shrine''' in Umahori is the second candidate shrine. Its former rank was mukakusha. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|qid=Q11059454|qid=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} === History === Its history is unclear. A tradition says the deity was moved from this site to the Yamamoto site. === Precinct === File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō umahori, Kameoka) honden.JPG|Protective housing over honden File:Kuwada-jinja (Shinochō umahori, Kameoka) torii.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrine === * Tenmangu === Access === * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|qid=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Sagano Line|Q1073011|qid=Q1073011|1=Sagano Line}} {{ill|lt=Umahori Station|Q1044103|qid=Q1044103|1=Umahori Station}} (about 3 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === {{Commons|Category:Kuwada-jinja (Kameoka)}} * On-site explanatory board (Yamamoto shrine only) * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Kyoto-fu no Chimei'' (Heibonsha), Kuwada Shrine entry * Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (ed.), ''Shikinaisha Chosa Hokoku'' Vol. 18, Kuwada Shrine entry {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kuwada Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kuwada Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Unranked Shrines in Kyōto Prefecture]] [[Category:丹波湖伝説]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kameoka, Kyoto]]
